get.description:
Retrieves all modules with credential requirements for a specific app and version.

For each module, this endpoint returns:
- Module identification (`id`, `name`, `label`)
- Required credential type (e.g., `account:slack2`)
- OAuth scopes required by the module
- Whether the module is a hook-based trigger

**Custom/SDK Apps**: To query custom (SDK) apps, prefix the app name with `app#` (URL-encoded as `app%23`).
For example: `/credential-requests/apps/app%23my-custom-app/1/modules-with-credentials`

parameters.name: name
parameters.description:
The name of the app. Must start with a lowercase letter, contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens, and end with a letter or number. Minimum 3 characters (e.g., `a-b`, `abc`).

For SDK/custom apps, use the `app#` prefix (URL-encoded as `app%23`).

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: The major version of the app. Use a specific version number (e.g., `1`, `2`, `4`) or `latest` to get the most recent version.

200.description: Successful response with list of app modules and their credential requirements.

x-imt-package-name.description: The name of the app package.

x-imt-package-version.description: The full version string of the app package.

appModules.description: List of modules with their credential requirements.

id.description:
Unique identifier for the module credential configuration.
For modules with a single credential, this matches the module name.
For modules with multiple credentials, includes additional context (e.g., `moduleName:paramName`).

name.description: The technical name of the module.

label.description: Human-readable display name of the module.

type.description:
The credential type required by the module.
Format is typically `account:<app-name>` or `keychain:<app-name>`.
Multiple types may be comma-separated (e.g., `account:slack2,slack3`).

scope.description: List of OAuth scopes required by this module.

hook.description: Indicates whether this module is a hook-based trigger.

slack-modules.description: Example response for Slack app version 4

message.description: Error message.

get.description: Gets a list of custom properties structures in the organization.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.

post.description:
Creates a custom properties structure. You can have only one custom properties structure for each combination of `associatedType`, `belongerType`and `belongerId` values.

For example, you can create only one custom properties structure for scenarios in a specific organization.

To create a structure for custom scenario properties, fill in the request body:

- `associatedType`: `scenario`
- `belongerType`: `organization`

Check out the example API call.

To define the custom properties structure items, use the API call [to create custom properties structure item](./structure-items/post--custom-property-structures--custompropertystructureid--custom-property-structure-items.md).

associatedType.description: The type of the entity which uses the custom properties structure. Fill in `scenario` to create custom scenario properties structure.

belongerType.description: The type of the entity that owns the custom properties structure. Fill in `organization` to create custom scenario properties structure.

belongerId.description: The ID of the entity that owns the custom properties structure.

get.description:
Gets the list of structure items in the specified custom properties structure.

Use the API call `GET /custom-property-structures?{organizationId}` to get the ID of the custom property structure.

parameters.name: customPropertyStructureId
parameters.description:
The ID of the custom property structure.
Use the API call `GET /custom-property-structures?organizationId={organizationId}` to get the ID of a custom property structure.

parameters.name: cols[]
parameters.description:
Specifies columns that are returned in the response. Use the `cols[]` parameter for every column that you want to return in the response. For example `GET /endpoint?cols[]=key1&cols[]=key2` to get both `key1` and `key2` columns in the response.

[Check the "Filtering" section for a full example.](/api-documentation/pagination-sorting-filtering/filtering)

parameters.name: pg[sortBy]
parameters.description: Specify the custom property item attribute. The custom property items in the response are sorted by the value of the attribute.

parameters.name: pg[offset]
parameters.description: The value of entities you want to skip before getting entities you need.

parameters.name: pg[sortDir]
parameters.description: The sorting order. It accepts the ascending and descending direction specifiers.

parameters.name: pg[limit]
parameters.description: Sets the maximum number of results per page in the API call response. For example, `pg[limit]=100`. The default number varies with different API endpoints.

customPropertyStructureItems.name: highPriority
customPropertyStructureItems.label: High Priority
customPropertyStructureItems.description: Specify whether the scenario is high priority or not.

customPropertyStructureItems.name: location
customPropertyStructureItems.label: Location
customPropertyStructureItems.description: Location of the team managing the scenario.

customPropertyStructureItems.name: performanceCheck
customPropertyStructureItems.label: Performance check due date
customPropertyStructureItems.description: The date when we should check whether the scenario remains valid and can be improved.

customPropertyStructureItems.name: companyTeam
customPropertyStructureItems.label: Company Team
customPropertyStructureItems.description: The team that manages the scenario

customPropertyStructureItems.name: category
customPropertyStructureItems.label: Category
customPropertyStructureItems.description: The category tags of the scenarios

post.summary: Create a custom property structure item
post.description:
Creates custom properties structure items.

After creating a structure item, you cannot change its `name` and `type`.

To add data to the custom properties, use the API call to [fill in custom properties data](../../scenarios/custom-properties-data/post--scenarios--scenarioid--custom-properties.md).

parameters.name: customPropertyStructureId
parameters.description:
The ID of the custom property structure.
Use the API call `GET /custom-property-structures?organizationId={organizationId}` to get the ID of a custom property structure.

name.description: The ID of the structure item. The `name` has to be unique in the custom properties structure.

label.description: Make displays the item label to users in the scenario table header.

description.description: The description of the custom property structure item. You can review the item description in the **Scenario properties** tab in the Organization dashboard.

type.description: The data type of the custom property structure item. The data types `dropdown` and `multiselect` allow you to specify available options for the item data.

options.description:
The options available to users when filling in the item data. For the data types `dropdown` and `multiselect`, fill in an object like `{"options":[{"value": "Marketing"}, {"value": "Sales"}]}`.
You can omit the `options` parameter for the rest of the data types.

required.description: Set to `true` in order to make a structure item required when adding custom property data. Default value is `false`.

basic-example.description: Add a custom properties structure item with the data type `shortText`.

value.name: teamLocation
value.label: Team location
value.description: Location of the team managing the scenario.

options-example.description: Add a custom properties structure item with a set of available options. The data `type` has to be `dropdown` or `multiselect`. Use the `options` attribute to specify available options.

customPropertyStructureItem.name: teamLocation
customPropertyStructureItem.label: Team location
customPropertyStructureItem.description: Location of the team managing the scenario.

patch.description:
Updates the specified item of a custom property structure. Specify the attributes you want to update in the request body.

You cannot update the item `name` and `type`.

You can get the item ID with an API call to [list custom property structure items](./get--custom-property-structures--custompropertystructureid--custom-property-structure-items.md).

When you want to update the `options` of a `multiselect` or `dropdown` item, specify all the options for the property. The new set of options replaces the current options.

parameters.name: customPropertyStructureItemId
parameters.description: The ID of the custom property structure item. Get the item ID with the API call to [list custom property structure items](/api-reference/custom-properties/structure-items/get--custom-properties--custompropertystructureid--custom-property-structure-items.md).

label.description: Make displays the item label to users in the scenario table header.

description.description: The description of the custom property structure item. You can review the item description in the **Scenario properties** tab in the Organization dashboard.

options.description:
The options available to users when filling in the item data. For the data types `dropdown` and `multiselect`, fill in an object like `{"options":[{"value": "Marketing"}, {"value": "Sales"}]}`.
You can omit the `options` parameter for the rest of the data types.

required.description: Set to `true` if you require to fill in data to the structure item when adding custom property data. Default value is `false`.

update-label.description: Updates the label of the custom property.

update-options.description: To update the custom property item options, specify the new set of available options. The new set of options replaces the previous options.

delete.description:
Deletes the specified custom property structure item. Use the `confirmed` parameter to confirm deleting the structure item.
When you delete a custom property item, Make deletes the data filled in the item as well. Deleting custom property item data is irreversible.

parameters.name: customPropertyStructureItemId
parameters.description: The ID of the custom property structure item. Get the item ID with the API call to [list custom property structure items](/api-reference/custom-properties/structure-items/get--custom-properties--custompropertystructureid--custom-property-structure-items.md).

get.description: Retrieves a collection of all data stores for a team with a given ID. Returned data stores are sorted by name in ascending order.

parameters.description: The unique ID of the team whose data stores will be retrieved.
parameters.name: teamId
cols[]
parameters.description: Specifies the group of values to return. For example, you can retrieve only names and IDs of data stores for a team with a given ID.
The value that will be used to sort returned entities by. Data stores can be currently sorted only by name.
parameters.name: pg[sortBy]
pg[offset]
parameters.description: The number of entities you want to skip before getting entities you want.

parameters.name: pg[sortDir]
parameters.description: The sorting order. It accepts the ascending and descending direction specifiers.

parameters.name: pg[limit]
parameters.description: The maximum number of entities you want to get in the response.

last.description: Shown only when using the `last` based pagination.

showLast.description: Shown only when using the `last` based pagination.

offset.description: Shown only when using the `offset` based pagination.

id-name.description: Returns IDs and names of data stores

post.description: Creates a new data store with data passed in the request body. In the response, it returns all details of the created data store.

name.description: The data store name. The name must be at most 128 characters long and does not need to be unique.

teamId.description: The unique ID of the team in which the data store will be created.

datastructureId.description: The unique ID of the data structure that will be included in the data store. All data structures IDs for a given team can be retrieved from the [List data structures](/api-reference/data-structures/get--data-structures.md) endpoint.

maxSizeMB.description: The maximum size of the data store (defined in MB).

delete.description: Deletes data stores with given IDs and returns their IDs in the response. This endpoint allows deleting one or more data stores at once.

parameters.description: Confirms the deletion if a data store is included in at least one scenario. Confirmation is required because the scenario will stop working without the data store. If the parameter is missing or it is set to `false` an error code is returned and the resource is not deleted.
parameters.name: confirmed

parameters.description: The unique ID of the team from which the data store will be deleted.
parameters.name: teamId

ids.description: The IDs of data stores to delete. You can either use only this parameter alone or use the `all` parameter, or the `all` parameter together with the `exceptIds` parameter.

exceptIds.description: The IDs of data stores to be excluded from deleting. It can be only used together with the `all` parameter set to `true`.

all.description: If set to `true`, all data stores will be deleted. It can be used alone or together with the `exceptIds` parameter.

get.description: Retrieves details of a data store with a given ID.

parameters.name: dataStoreId
parameters.description: The ID of the data store. Get the `dataStoreId` from the [List data stores](/api-reference/data-stores/get--data-stores.md) endpoint.

parameters.name: cols[]
parameters.description: Specifies the group of values to return. For example, you can retrieve only names and IDs of data stores for a team with a given ID.

patch.description: Updates properties of a data store with a given ID in a team with a given ID by passing new values in the request body. In the response, it returns all details of the updated data store including properties that were not changed. Any property that is not provided will be left unchanged. This endpoint cannot be used to update data included in the data store - for this use the [endpoints for managing data stores data](./data/get--data-stores--datastoreid--data.md).

name.description: The data store name. The name must be at most 128 characters long and does not need to be unique.

datastructureId.description: The unique ID of the data structure included in the data store. All data structures IDs for a given team can be retrieved from the [List data structures](/api-reference/data-structures/get--data-structures.md) endpoint.

maxSizeMB.description: The maximum size of the data store (defined in MB).

parameters.name: dataStoreId
parameters.description: The ID of the data store. Get the `dataStoreId` from the [List data stores](/api-reference/data-stores/get--data-stores.md) endpoint.

get.description: Retrieves a collection of all records from a data store with a given ID. Each returned record consists of the `key` (custom or automatically generated) and `data`.

parameters.name: dataStoreId
parameters.description: The ID of the data store. Get the `dataStoreId` from the [List data stores](/api-reference/data-stores/get--data-stores.md) endpoint.

delete.description:
Deletes records from the specified data store.

Specify the `keys` of the records you want to delete in the `keys` array in the request body.

Use the `all` parameter in the request body to delete all records from the data store. You have to add the `confirmed` parameter in the API call query to confirm deleting the data, otherwise you get an error.

When you are deleting all records, you can use the `exceptKeys` parameter to specify keys of the records which you want to keep.

The response contains keys of the deleted records.

parameters.name: dataStoreId
parameters.description: The ID of the data store. Get the `dataStoreId` from the [List data stores](/api-reference/data-stores/get--data-stores.md) endpoint.
Set to `true` to confirm deleting of the data store records. Otherwise, you get an error and Make won't delete the data store records.
parameters.name: confirmed

keys.description: The keys of data store records you want to delete. Use the `all` and `confirmed` parameters if you want to delete all records in the data store.

all.description: Set to `true` to delete all records in the data store. Use the `confirmed` parameter to confirm the deletion. You can also use the `exceptKeys` parameter to specify keys of the records that you want to keep in the data store.

exceptKeys.description: Specify the keys of the data store records you want to keep when deleting all records from the data store.

delete-selected.description: Delete records in the data store. Specify the record keys in the `keys` array in the request body.

delete-all.description: Deletes all records in the data store. You have to confirm the deletion with the `confirmed` parameter. You can use the `exceptKeys` parameter to specify keys of the records you want to keep.

default.description: Returns data structures with empty specification

all.description: Returns data structure with full specification

post.description: Creates a new data structure with data passed in the request body. In the response, it returns all details of the created data structure including its full specification.

teamId.description: The unique ID of the team in which the data structure will be created.

name.description: The name of the data structure. The maximum length of the name is 128 characters.

strict.description:
Set to `true` to enforce strict validation of the data put in the data structure. With the strict validation enabled, the data structure won't store data that don't fit into the structure and the storing module will return an error.

The default value of this parameter is `false`. With the default setting, the modules using the data structure will process data that don't conform to the data structure.

get.description: Retrieves a data structure with a given ID

parameters.name: cols[]
parameters.description: Specifies the group of values to return. For example, you can retrieve only name of a data structure.

patch.description:
Updates the specified data structure. Make updates only parameters you send in the request body.
Note that when you update the data structure specification with the `spec` parameter, you have to provide all structure fields you want to use. Make replaces the old structure specification with the new one.
The response contains all details about the updated data structure.

get.description: Required scope: devices:read
Retrieves the list of incomplete executions of the specified scenario.

parameters.name: scenarioId
parameters.description: The ID value of the scenario. Use the API call `GET /scenarios` to get the ID of the scenario. If your scenario is placed in a folder, use the API call `GET /scenarios-folders?teamId={teamId}` first.

delete.description:
Deletes incomplete executions of the specified scenario. Specify the incomplete execution ID values in the `ids` array in the request body to delete the specified incomplete executions.

You can set the `"all": true` pair to delete all incomplete executions of the specified scenario. If you use the `"all": true` parameter, you have to specify the `confirmed=true` query parameter to confirm the deletion. Otherwise, the API call returns the error IM004 (406).

Add the `exceptIds` array to the request body to specify items you don't want to delete.

You get an error if you try to delete incomplete executions which are being processed. The rest of the specified items is still deleted.

parameters.name: scenarioId
parameters.description: The ID value of the scenario. Use the API call `GET /scenarios` to get the ID of the scenario. If your scenario is placed in a folder, use the API call `GET /scenarios-folders?teamId={teamId}` first.

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Set to `true` to confirm deleting the incomplete executions. Otherwise the API call fails with the error IM004 (406).

ids.description: The ID values of the scenario incomplete executions that you want to delete. Use the API call `GET /dlqs/?scenarioId={scenarioId}` to get the ID values of the webhook processing queue items.

exceptIds.description: If you are deleting all of the incomplete executions with the `all:true` parameter, you can specify the ID values of the incomplete executions that you want to keep. Use the API call `GET /dlqs?scenarioId={scenarioId}` to get the ID values of the incomplete executions.

all.description: Set to `true` to delete all incomplete executions of the specified scenario.

delete.description: Delete incomplete executions of the specified scenario.

delete-with-error.description: You get the API error if you try to delete incomplete executions that are being processed at the moment. The items that are not being processed will be deleted.

The status number of the incomplete execution. The status numbers correspond to the following statuses:

- 1: success,
 - 2: warning,
 - 3: error.

parameters.description: The moment from which you want to list the incomplete execution logs. The timestamp is in the [UNIX timestamp](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unix_time) format.

parameters.name: to

get.description: Gets detail of the specified incomplete execution log.

parameters.name: dlqId

post.description:
Triggers a retry of the specified incomplete execution. The incomplete execution runs with the blueprint from when the error happened.

If you need to update the blueprint first, use the endpoint `PATCH /dlqs/{dlqId}`.

parameters.name: dlqId
parameters.description: The ID of the incomplete execution. Get the ID values of the incomplete executions of a scenario with the API call `GET /dlqs?scenarioId={scenarioId}`.

post.description:
Triggers a retry of the specified incomplete executions. You can either use the `all` parameter to retry all incomplete executions of the scenario, or specify a list of incomplete execution IDs in the `ids` property.

You can use the `exceptIds` parameter to exclude incomplete executions from retrying.

The incomplete executions run with the blueprint from when the error happened. If you need to update the blueprint first, use the endpoint `PATCH /dlqs/{dlqId}`.

Make puts the incomplete executions in a queue. If you are retrying a large number of incomplete executions, there might be a delay between receiving the response and Make retrying the incomplete execution.

parameters.name: scenarioId
parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. You can get the `scenarioId` with the [List scenarios](/scenarios-get) API call.

ids.description: The list of incomplete execution IDs you want to retry. All of the IDs have to belong to the same scenario.

all.description: Set to `true` to retry all incomplete executions of the scenario.

exceptIds.description: You can use this parameter together with the `all` parameter to specify incomplete execution IDs which shouldn't be retried.

specify-ids.description: Retry multiple incomplete executions of a scenario. Specify the incomplete execution IDs in the `dlqs` array.

all-except.description: Retry all incomplete executions of a scenario except for one.

get.description: Returns historical parallel execution usage metrics for an Organization over a time range, aggregated into samples at a fixed rollup interval. The sample with the highest capacity utilization is picked for each time interval.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization to get execution capacity metrics for

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of the time range (inclusive), as an ISO 8601 date-time

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of the time range (exclusive), as an ISO 8601 date-time

parameters.name: rollupSeconds
parameters.description: Time interval in seconds used for rolling up the samples. When omitted, a rollup is chosen automatically so the sample count stays at constant size.

rollupSeconds.description: Time interval in seconds that was used for rolling up the samples

samples.description: Slot usage samples, one per rollup interval in the requested range

scenarioIds.description: IDs of the Scenarios executing while this sample was taken

slots.description: Number of slots used per Scenario, positionally aligned with scenarioIds

totalSlots.description: Total number of slots used across all Scenarios in this sample

limit.description: The Organization's parallel execution slot limit at the time of the sample

default.summary: One hour of usage rolled up into 60-second samples

422.description: The requested range and rollup would yield too many samples

get.description: Returns a single, real-time snapshot of parallel execution slot usage for an Organization — how many execution slots are currently acquired per scenario, the total slots in use, and the Organization's slot limit. Unlike the historical metrics endpoint, this reflects the live state at the moment of the request.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the Organization to get the live execution capacity snapshot for

snapshotAt.description: Timestamp at which the snapshot was taken

scenarioIds.description: IDs of the Scenarios that currently have slots used

slots.description: Number of slots currently used per Scenario, positionally aligned with scenarioIds

totalSlots.description: Total number of slots currently used across all Scenarios

limit.description: The Organization's parallel execution slot limit

get.description:
Retrieves a list of custom functions available in the team. The response contains `id`, `name`, `description` and a brief updates history of all custom functions available in the team.

Check availability of the custom functions feature with the API call `GET /organizations/{organizationId}` for the organization to which the team belongs. If the response contains `"customFunctions": true` pair in the `license` object then you have access to the custom functions feature.

Refer to the [Make pricing page](https://www.make.com/en/help/general/pricing-parameters) for Make pricing plans overview.

parameters.name: cols[]
parameters.description:
Specifies columns that are returned in the response. Use the `cols[]` parameter for every column that you want to return in the response. For example `GET /endpoint?cols[]=key1&cols[]=key2` to get both `key1` and `key2` columns in the response.

[Check the "Filtering" section for a full example.](/api-documentation/pagination-sorting-filtering/filtering)

functions.name: checkType
functions.description: Returns the data type of the argument.

functions.name: numberOfWorkingDays
functions.description: Calculate how many working days are between the two provided dates.

post.description:
Creates a custom function. Specify function `name`, `description` and `code` in the request body.

You cannot use a JavaScript reserved word for the function `name`. Check the [list of JavaScript reserved words](https://www.w3schools.com/js/js_reserved.asp).

Make sure to use the same function name in the `name` field and in the function's `code`. Otherwise, you get the IM005 error.

Make validates the custom function's code first. You get an IM005 error if the code validation fails. The validation might fail because of a syntax error in the function's code or when the code uses a JavaScript feature that Make doesn't support. Check the [custom functions limitations](https://www.make.com/en/help/functions/custom-functions#limitations-of-custom-functions) in the Make Help center.

Check availability of the custom functions feature with the API call `GET /organizations/{organizationId}` for the organization to which the team belongs. If the response contains `"customFunctions": true` pair in the `license` object then you have access to the custom functions feature.

Refer to the [Make pricing page](https://www.make.com/en/help/general/pricing-parameters) for Make pricing plans overview.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The ID of the team.

name.description: The name of the custom function.

description.description: The description of the custom function.

code.description: The code of the custom function.

example.name: symmetricDifference
example.description: A function which returns an array with items unique across two arrays.
example.code: function symmetricDifference(array1, array2){difference = array1.filter(x => !array2.includes(x)).concat(array2.filter(x => !array1.includes(x))) return difference}

function.name: symmetricDifference
function.description: A function which returns an array with items unique across two arrays.
function.code: function symmetricDifference(array1, array2){difference = array1.filter(x => !array2.includes(x)).concat(array2.filter(x => !array1.includes(x))) return difference}

post.description:
Checks the custom functions code. The response contains information whether Make validated the custom functions code successfully or whether there was an error.

The code validation might fail because of an syntax error in the function's code or when the code uses a JavaScript feature that Make doesn't support. Check the [custom functions limitations](https://www.make.com/en/help/functions/custom-functions#limitations-of-custom-functions) in the Make Help center.

Check availability of the custom functions feature with the API call `GET /organizations/{organizationId}` for the organization to which the team belongs. If the response contains `"customFunctions": true` pair in the `license` object then you have access to the custom functions feature.

Refer to the [Make pricing page](https://www.make.com/en/help/general/pricing-parameters) for Make pricing plans overview.

get.description: Pings the Make API service. Successful response contains plain text.
Retrieves a collection of all hooks for a team with a given ID. Returned hooks are sorted by ID in ascending order by default. Use `pg[sortBy]` to sort by `name` or `hookName`.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The unique ID of the team whose hooks will be retrieved.

parameters.name: typeName
parameters.description: The hook type. Two native Make hook types are `gateway-webhook` and `gateway-mailhook`.

parameters.name: assigned
parameters.description: Specifies if the hook is assigned to a scenario. If set to `true`, the request will return only the hooks which the `scenarioId` value is not set to null.

parameters.name: viewForScenarioId
parameters.description: This parameter shows only the hooks that can be used by a scenario with a specific ID, which means hooks that are not assigned to another scenario yet and the hook that is already assigned to this scenario. This can be useful because Make allows assigning any hook to only one scenario. If this parameter is set the `assigned` parameter is ignored.

post.description: Creates a new hook with data passed in the request body. In the response, it returns all details of the created hook.

name.description: The name of the hook. The name must be at most 128 characters long and does not need to be unique.

teamId.description: The unique ID of the team in which a hook will be created.

typeName.description: The hook type strictly related to the app for which the hook was created.

method.description: Set the `method` parameter to `true` to add the HTTP method to the request body.

headers.description: Set the `headers` parameter to `true` to add headers to the request body.

stringify.description: Set the `stringify` parameter to `true` to return JSON payloads as strings.

__IMTCONN__.description: The unique ID of the connection that will be included in the created hook.

formId.description: The unique ID of the form that will be included in the created hook.

get.description: Retrieves details of a hook with a given ID including hooks data.

parameters.name: hookId
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook. Use the `GET /hooks` API call to get the ID values of your webhooks.

delete.description: Deletes a hook with a given ID and returns the ID in the response.

parameters.name: hookId
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook. Use the `GET /hooks` API call to get the ID values of your webhooks.

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Confirms the deletion if a hook is included in the scenario. Confirmation is required because the scenario will stop working without the hook. If the parameter is missing or it is set to `false` an error code is returned and the resource is not deleted.

patch.description: Updates a hook with a given ID by passing new values in the request body. Any property that is not provided will be left unchanged. In the response, it returns all details of the updated hook including properties that were not changed.

name.description: The name of the updated hook. The name must be at most 128 characters long and does not need to be unique.

parameters.name: hookId
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook. Use the `GET /hooks` API call to get the ID values of your webhooks.

get.description: Determines if a hook with a given ID is active and retrieves its properties that provide you with the address of the hook and inform if the hook is attached, what is its learning status, and if it was not used for a long time.

parameters.name: hookId
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook. Use the `GET /hooks` API call to get the ID values of your webhooks.

dataStructure.description: List of fields parsed from the last received sample. Null if the payload is not yet determined or if the hook is currently in learning mode.

post.description: Starts the process of learning the request body structure by a hook with a given ID. When you send to the hook address a request with data in its body, you can use this endpoint to force the hook to start determining the payload data structure which will later be suggested in the scenario as the output of the hook. The data structure learning process also starts automatically when a new hook is created and stops once the data structure is determined. If you want to stop this process, you can use the [Learn stop](./post--hooks--hookid--learn-stop.md) endpoint.

parameters.name: hookId
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook. Use the `GET /hooks` API call to get the ID values of your webhooks.

post.description: Stops the process of learning the request body structure by a hook with a given ID. When you send to the hook address a request with data in its body, you can use the [Learn start](./post--hooks--hookid--learn-start.md) endpoint to force the hook to start determining the payload data structure which will later be suggested in the scenario as the output of the hook. The data structure learning process also starts automatically when a new hook is created and stops once the data structure is determined. You can use this endpoint to stop the learning process at any time.

parameters.name: hookId
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook. Use the `GET /hooks` API call to get the ID values of your webhooks.

post.description: Enables a disabled hook with a given ID. Newly created hooks are enabled by default which means they are ready to accept data. In response, this endpoint returns the confirmation that the hook was successfully enabled.

parameters.name: hookId
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook. Use the `GET /hooks` API call to get the ID values of your webhooks.

post.description: Disables a hook with a given ID. Newly created hooks are enabled by default which means they are ready to accept data. The disabled hook does not accept any data. This endpoint can be useful when you want to debug the scenario functionality. In response, this endpoint returns the confirmation that the hook was successfully disabled.

parameters.name: hookId
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook. Use the `GET /hooks` API call to get the ID values of your webhooks.

post.description: Sets data for a hook with a given ID. Data differ depending on the hook type. It returns the confirmation if the hook data was changed (`true`) or not (`false`).

parameters.name: hookId
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook. Use the `GET /hooks` API call to get the ID values of your webhooks.

get.description:
Retrieves a list of webhook queue items waiting for processing with the specified webhook. The request response contains:

- the incoming payload hash
 - incoming data scope
 - the size of the data in bytes
 - timestamp of the moment when the data were placed in the processing queue

parameters.name: hookId
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook. Use the `GET /hooks` API call to get the ID values of your webhooks.

parameters.name: pg[sortBy]
parameters.description: The value that will be used to sort returned entities by.

parameters.name: pg[offset]
parameters.description: The value of entities you want to skip before getting entities you need.

parameters.name: pg[sortDir]
parameters.description: The sorting order. It accepts the ascending and descending direction specifiers.

parameters.name: pg[limit]
parameters.description: Sets the maximum number of results per page in the API call response. For example, `pg[limit]=100`. The default number varies with different API endpoints.

delete.description:
Deletes items from the processing queue of the specified webhook. Specify the payload ID values that you want to delete in the request body in the `ids` array.

You can set the `"all": true` pair to delete all items from the webhook processing queue. If you use the `"all": true` parameter, you have to specify the `confirmed=true` query parameter to confirm the deletion. Otherwise the API call returns the error IM004 (406).

Add the `exceptIds` array to the request body to specify items you don't want to delete.

The API call response will contain an error message if some of the specified queue items cannot be deleted. The rest of the specified items will be deleted. Deleting a webhook queue item is not allowed when the item is already being processed by the webhook.

parameters.name: hookId
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook. Use the `GET /hooks` API call to get the ID values of your webhooks.
Set to `true` to confirm deleting the webhook queue items. Otherwise the API call fails with the error IM004 (406).
parameters.name: confirmed

ids.description: The ID values of the webhook processing queue items that you want to delete. Use the API call `GET /hooks/{hookId}/incomings` to get the ID values of the webhook processing queue items.

exceptIds.description: If you are deleting all of the incomplete executions with the `all:true` parameter, you can specify the ID values of the webhook queue items that you want to keep. Use the API call `GET /hooks/{hookId}/incomings` to get the ID values of the webhook queue items.

all.description: Set to `true` to delete all items in the webhook processing queue.

delete.description: Delete webhook queue items.

delete-with-error.description: The API call response will contain an error message if you try to delete webhook queue items that Make is processing at the moment. The rest of the incomplete execution items will be deleted.

error.name: APIError
error.message: Some of the incoming messages could not be deleted because they are being processed right now.

detail-1.description: Detail of a webhook queue item that contains data in the JSON data format.

get.description:
Retrieves the specified webhook execution log. The response contains:

- `statusId`: the status of the webhook execution; `1` means successful execution, `3` means failed execution
 - `loggedAt`: the moment when Make created the log
 - `id`: the ID of the webhook execution log
 - `data`: information about the request, header, and payload sent to the webhook.

Some webhooks don't return the `data` object due to their implementation. Most common examples include instant triggers for Slack, Zoom, Intercom and Facebook lead ads apps.

get.summary: List of apps+modules available to the current user for the scenario editor app search.
get.description:
Returns the apps catalog used by the scenario editor's app search. Two response shapes are
supported via the `scoredSearch` query flag:

- **Default (no `scoredSearch`)** — flat list of `SearchAppVersion` entries (one per native/SDK
  package) plus `AddonApp` entries.
- **`scoredSearch=true`** — apps are scored against zone-level and team-level usage to rank
  common picks higher. Each app aggregates its versions and modules under
  `versions[].modules[]`, and `versions[].groups[]` carries the rendered grouping for the UI.

The scored response is transformed before being returned:

1. **Group normalization** — `versions[].groups` is normalized so the response always exposes
   a well-formed grouping. Authored groupings with fewer than 2 groups are discarded and
   replaced with per-type default groups (`'actions'`, `'triggers'`, …); otherwise authored
   groups are preserved and orphan modules (those not referenced by any group) are appended
   to per-type defaults.
2. **Module reorganization** — modules can be copied or moved across apps according to
   product-managed rules. A relocated module appears in the target app's `versions[].modules[]`
   carrying `appName`, `appVersion`, and `theme` fields that point at its **source** package.
   The frontend must use these fields (when present) to load the correct source manifest and
   render the module in its original app's branding, because a module's unique identity is
   `(parentApp.name, module.name)`.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: Organization context for filtering custom (SDK) apps and resolving feature controls.

parameters.name: scoredSearch
parameters.description:
If `true`, the response is enriched with scoring (zone + team usage) and modules are
aggregated per version. Requires `teamId`. When omitted, a non-scored shape is returned.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description:
Team whose usage drives team-level module scoring. **Required when `scoredSearch=true`**;
ignored otherwise.

oneOf.description:
A native or SDK app with version/module aggregation and zone+team scoring applied.
Returned only when `scoredSearch=true`.

name.description: App package name.

label.description: App display label.

theme.description: Hex color of the app's branding (e.g. `'#18bfff'`).

premiumTier.description: Premium level [0-10]; SDK apps default to 0.

type.description: App kind. Addon apps use the `AddonApp` schema.

score.description: Aggregate score combining zone- and team-level usage.

version.description: Full semver version of the package (e.g. `'1.2.0'`).

foreign.description: True when the app is installed from an invite link.

items.description:
A module under a scored app version. May be either a native module of the parent app, or
a module **relocated** from a different source app by the product-managed reorganizer.
When relocated, `appName`/`appVersion`/`theme` identify the *source* package — see the
endpoint description.

type.description: Module kind (e.g. `'action'`, `'trigger'`, `'search'`, …).

appName.description:
Present only on **relocated** modules. Name of the source app package this module
originally belongs to. Frontend MUST use this (when present) as the package identifier
when materializing the module into a scenario blueprint.

appVersion.description:
Present only on relocated modules. Full semver version of the source package
(e.g. `'1.0.0'`). Used together with `appName` to load the source manifest.

theme.description:
Present only on relocated modules. Hex color theme of the source package, so the
frontend can render the module in its original app's branding.

groups.description:
Always present after the group normalization step (see endpoint description).
Either authored groups (≥ 2 of them) plus any per-type defaults for orphan modules,
or per-type default groups if the app had no real authored grouping.

label.description:
Authored group label, or a per-type default like `'actions'`, `'triggers'`,
`'searches'`, `'transformers'`, `'routers'`, etc.

modules.description: Module names belonging to this group (references into `modules[]`).

oneOf.description:
Addon entries returned alongside native/SDK apps. Shape is preserved from
`req.app.get('addonApps')` (provided by the `load-addon.apps.js` loader).

apps.description:
When `scoredSearch=true`, items are `ScoredSearchApp` (native/SDK) or
`AddonApp`. When `scoredSearch` is omitted/false, items are the non-scored
manifest shapes (not documented here).

A native or SDK app with version/module aggregation and zone+team scoring applied.
Returned only when `scoredSearch=true`.

get.summary: List the endpoints usable in a team given its available apps and connections.
get.description:
Returns the catalog of app **endpoints** a team can actually call right now, considering both the
apps available to the team's organization and the connections (accounts) that exist in the team.
Covers both native (compiled) packages and SDK apps; results are grouped by package and version.

An endpoint is included when:

- its app is available to the team's organization (installed / public), **and**
- either it requires no connection at all (no attached account — usable without authentication), or
  the team has at least one connection of a matching account type whose granted OAuth scopes cover
  the scopes the endpoint requires.

For each returned endpoint, `credentialsRequired` indicates whether the endpoint needs a connection at
all, and `credentialsAvailable` lists the concrete connections in the team that satisfy it (empty for
endpoints that need no connection). Private and deprecated endpoints are excluded, except that the
app's author always sees their own (non-public and deprecated) endpoints. By default the caller's
pending annotation changes are reflected for endpoints of apps they author.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The team to list usable endpoints for. The caller needs the `accounts:read` team permission.

packages.description: Native packages and SDK apps, each with its usable endpoints grouped by version.

name.description: Package name. SDK apps are prefixed with `app#` (e.g. `app#my-crm`).

label.description: Package display label.

annotations.description:
MCP-style behavior hints (e.g. `readOnlyHint`, `destructiveHint`). For
endpoints of an app the caller authors, this reflects the caller's pending
annotation changes.

deprecated.description: Whether the endpoint is deprecated. Deprecated endpoints are normally omitted.

private.description:
Whether the endpoint is non-public. Non-public endpoints are visible only to
the app author.

credentialsRequired.description:
Whether the endpoint requires a connection (account) to be called. `false` for
endpoints usable without authentication.

credentialsAvailable.description:
The connections in the team that can drive this endpoint. Empty for endpoints
that need no connection (usable without authentication).

name.description: Connection display name.

get.description:
**Deprecated.** Use `GET /credential-requests/apps/{name}/{version}/modules-with-credentials` instead.
This endpoint continues to work but will be removed in a future release.

Retrieves all modules with credential requirements for a specific app and version.

For each module, this endpoint returns:
- Module identification (`id`, `name`, `label`)
- Required credential type (e.g., `account:slack2`)
- OAuth scopes required by the module
- Whether the module is a hook-based trigger

**Custom/SDK Apps**: To query custom (SDK) apps, prefix the app name with `app#` (URL-encoded as `app%23`).
For example: `/imt/apps/app%23my-custom-app/1/modules-with-credentials`

parameters.name: name
parameters.description:
The name of the app. Must start with a lowercase letter, contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens, and end with a letter or number. Minimum 3 characters (e.g., `a-b`, `abc`).

For SDK/custom apps, use the `app#` prefix (URL-encoded as `app%23`).

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: The major version of the app. Use a specific version number (e.g., `1`, `2`, `4`) or `latest` to get the most recent version.

200.description: Successful response with list of app modules and their credential requirements.

x-imt-package-name.description: The name of the app package.

x-imt-package-version.description: The full version string of the app package.

appModules.description: List of modules with their credential requirements.

id.description:
Unique identifier for the module credential configuration.
For modules with a single credential, this matches the module name.
For modules with multiple credentials, includes additional context (e.g., `moduleName:paramName`).

name.description: The technical name of the module.

label.description: Human-readable display name of the module.

type.description:
The credential type required by the module.
Format is typically `account:<app-name>` or `keychain:<app-name>`.
Multiple types may be comma-separated (e.g., `account:slack2,slack3`).

scope.description: List of OAuth scopes required by this module.

hook.description: Indicates whether this module is a hook-based trigger.

slack-modules.description: Example response for Slack app version 4

message.description: Error message.

get.summary: Internal - Get organization details by IDs
get.description: Retrieves details for specified organizations by their IDs. You can specify which columns to return using the `cols[]` query parameter. If `cols[]` is not provided, all available details for the organizations are returned.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: An array of organization IDs to retrieve details for.

parameters.name: cols
parameters.description: An array of column names to include in the response for each organization. If omitted, all available columns are returned.

items.description: Represents an organization's details. The actual fields returned depend on the 'cols' parameter or all fields if 'cols' is omitted.

id.description: The organization's unique identifier.

name.description: The name of the organization.

created.description: The creation timestamp of the organization.

updated.description: The last update timestamp of the organization.

deleted.description: Flag indicating if the organization is deleted.

deletedAt.description: The timestamp when the organization was deleted.

countryId.description: The country ID for the organization.

initialTeamId.description: This is the ID of the first team that is created in the organization. There is no foreign key on purpose, since the reference can become invalid when the initial team is deleted. On the other hand, since process of creating a new organization and a new, first team in the organization, can be a bit decoupled, we need to set the value only when we really know that no team has been created so far. That is why we keep the dangling reference, because when the field is not null, but contains any number, we know that at least one team has been already created.

productName.description: Product name details, typically a JSON object or string.

rev.description: Revision number of the organization record.

ssoType.description: Single Sign-On type configured for the organization.

timezoneId.description: The timezone ID for the organization.

userSessionTimeout.description: User session timeout in seconds for the organization.

get.description:
Returns a list of scenarios belonging to a team for ISC consumers (e.g. make-adapter). Requires a valid ISC JWT signed by the caller's shared secret.

**Full mode** (no `updatedSince`): returns all non-deleted, non-tool scenarios.

**Delta mode** (`updatedSince` provided as epoch-ms): returns every scenario whose `lastEdit` is >= that timestamp, including soft-deleted ones (`isDeleted: true`). Soft-deletes bump `lastEdit`, so deletions ride the same delta response without a separate reconcile pass.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: Numeric ID of the team whose scenarios to list.

parameters.name: updatedSince
parameters.description: Optional epoch-ms timestamp. When provided, switches to delta mode: returns all scenarios (including soft-deleted) whose lastEdit >= this value.

scenarios.description: Scenarios for the team. In full mode, only non-deleted scenarios are included. In delta mode, soft-deleted scenarios are also returned with isDeleted: true.

id.description: Scenario ID.

name.description: Scenario display name.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last edit.

isDeleted.description: Whether the scenario has been soft-deleted. Always false in full mode. In delta mode, true means the scenario was deleted since the last sync.

400.description: Missing or invalid teamId or updatedSince query parameter.

post.description: Creates a new team connection (TAC) for the specified team. Non-OAuth connections are automatically tested after creation via the ConnectionWorker validate/test lifecycle — on success the connection is marked as valid and credentials are persisted. If the test fails, the connection is deleted and a 422 is returned. OAuth connections are created but left unauthorized — the caller must complete the OAuth flow separately.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The ID of the team to create the connection for.

accountType.description: The account type identifier, usually same as accountName.

userId.description: The ID of the user creating the connection. Must exist and have 'account add' permission on the team.

language.description: Language code for localized labels (e.g. 'en', 'cs'). Defaults to 'en'.

200.description: Connection created (and tested for non-OAuth types) successfully.

400.description: Invalid request — missing required fields or invalid account type.

403.description: User does not have permission to create connections on this team, or team does not exist.

422.description: Connection was created but failed verification — the connection has been deleted.

However, the values for parameters `license`, `serviceName` and `isPaused` are returned only for organizations in your current Make zone.

Returned organizations are sorted by the organization name in ascending order by default. You can specify sorting order with the query parameter `pg[sortBy].`

Specifies columns that are returned in the response. Use the `cols[]` parameter for every column that you want to return in the response. For example `GET /endpoint?cols[]=key1&cols[]=key2` to get both `key1` and `key2` columns in the response.

[Check the "Filtering" section for a full example.](/api-documentation/pagination-sorting-filtering/filtering)

get.summary: Get detail of an active subscription

pauseStartsAt.description: When the pause becomes effective (end of the current paid period). Null when not paused.

pauseEndsAt.description: Scheduled auto-resume date. Null when not paused.

isSubscriptionPaused.description: True while the pause is in effect (subscription.pause_applied is true).

post.description:
Schedules a pause for the active monthly self-service subscription.
The pause becomes effective at the end of the current paid period and
is automatically resumed on `resumeAt`. While paused the organization
is treated as unlicensed (0 credits) and Stripe collection is suspended.

resumeAt.description: Date when the subscription should automatically resume. Must be after the current period end and within 12 months.

post.summary: Resume the subscription (cancel scheduled pause or resume now)
post.description:
Ends the pause for the subscription. If the pause is still scheduled
(current period not yet ended) it is simply cancelled — no charge,
no billing reset. If the pause is in effect, the subscription is
resumed immediately, billing anchor is reset to now and a fresh
period is charged.

get.description:
Retrieves the collection of organization variables. The response contains all organization variables if your account has the custom variables feature available. Otherwise, the response contains only Make system variables.

Check availability of the custom variables feature with the API call `GET /organizations/{organizationId}`. If the response contains `"customVariables": true` pair in the `license` object then you have access to the custom variables feature.

Refer to the [Make pricing page](https://www.make.com/en/help/general/pricing-parameters) for Make pricing plans overview.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.

post.description:
Creates new organization variable. You can check the availability of the custom variables feature with the API call `GET /organizations/{organizationId}`. If the response contains `"customVariables": true` pair in the `license` object then you have access to the custom variables feature.

A successful response contains all information about the new variable. If you don't have the custom variables feature available then the API call returns the error 404.

Refer to the [Make pricing page](https://www.make.com/en/help/general/pricing-parameters) for Make pricing plans overview.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.

typeId.description:
Number representing the type of the custom variable. The mapping of `typeId` and variable types is as follows:

- 1: `number`,
 - 2: `string`,
 - 3: `boolean`,
 - 4: `date`in ISO 8601 compliant format `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ`. For example: `1998-03-06T12:31:00.000Z`.

value.description: Value assigned to the custom variable.

name.description: The name of the variable. You can use letters, digits, `$` and `_` characters in the custom variable name.

patch.description:
Updates custom organization variable with the specified variable `name`. Only parameters specified in the request body are updated.

You can check the availability of the custom variables feature with the API call `GET /organizations/{organizationId}`. If the response contains `"customVariables": true` pair in the `license` object then you have access to the custom variables feature.

A successful response contains all information about the updated variable. If you don't have the custom variables feature available then the API call returns the error 404.

Refer to the [Make pricing page](https://www.make.com/en/help/general/pricing-parameters) for the Make pricing plans overview.

Update the variable `typeId` accordingly when you are updating the variable `value`. Make checks whether the variable type and value match in the request body. There is no check for incorrect variable type when you update only variable value and _vice versa_.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.

parameters.name: variableName
parameters.description: The name of the custom variable.

typeId.description:
Number representing the type of the custom variable. The mapping of `typeId` and variable types is as follows:

- 1: `number`,
 - 2: `string`,
 - 3: `boolean`,
 - 4: `date`in ISO 8601 compliant format `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ`. For example: `1998-03-06T12:31:00.000Z`.

value.description: Value assigned to the custom variable.

delete.description:
Deletes organization variable.

If you don't have the custom variables feature available then the API call returns the error 404. Check the availability of the custom variables feature with the API call `GET /organizations/{organizationId}`. If the response contains `"customVariables": true` pair in the `license` object then you have access to the custom variables feature.

Refer to the [Make pricing page](https://www.make.com/en/help/general/pricing-parameters) for Make pricing plans overview.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.

parameters.name: variableName
parameters.description: The name of the custom variable.

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Set to `true` to confirm deleting the custom variable. Otherwise the API call fails with the error IM004 (406).

get.description:
Gets the history of updates of the specified custom variable. The response contains the ID value of the variable history entry and a `diffObject`. The `diffObject` contains:

- the original value,
 - the new value,
 - the timestamp of the update,
 - the author of the update.

If the variable doesn't have any updates, the `diffObject` contains the current value of the variable instead of the original and new values.

The update history entries are sorted from newest to latest.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.

parameters.name: variableName
parameters.description: The name of the custom variable.

get.description:
Retrieves a list of daily centicredits, operations, and data transfer usage across all scenarios within all teams in the specified organization for the past 30 days.

By default, the endpoint uses the timezone of the user making the API call to define the start and end of each day in the 30-day timeframe.

To use the organization's timezone instead, set the `organizationTimezone` parameter to `true`. This ensures that the daily aggregates align with the organization's operational hours. This is especially useful for scenarios where aggregated data needs to align with the organization's operational hours.

For instance, a remote data analyst in India working for a Czech company can set `organizationTimezone=true` to ensure the usage data reflects the company's timezone, providing more relevant and accurate insights for organizational reporting and analysis.

For more information on timezones in Make, please refer to our [Help Center article](https://www.make.com/en/help/general/manage-time-zones).

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.

parameters.name: organizationTimezone
parameters.description: When set to `true`, the endpoint will calculate and return usage data based on the organization's timezone instead of the user's local timezone.

post.description:
Retrieves execution logs across all scenarios in an organization (or a specific team within the organization), narrowed by `from`/`to`, execution `status`, `scenarioIds`, or a substring of the `errorMessage`.

When `from` or `to` are omitted, the time window defaults to the last 7 days. Results are returned in reverse-chronological order.

**The `from` and `to` bounds are applied at UTC-day granularity** — only the calendar-day portion of each timestamp is honored. Executions that occurred earlier or later within the same UTC day as a supplied bound may still appear in the results.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.

from.description:
Lower bound of the time window, as a millisecond Unix timestamp. Defaults to 7 days before `to` when omitted.

Applied at **UTC-day granularity**: the calendar day of this value is the earliest day searched, but executions earlier within that same day may still be returned.

to.description:
Upper bound of the time window, as a millisecond Unix timestamp. Defaults to the current time when omitted.

Applied at **UTC-day granularity**: the calendar day of this value is the latest day searched, but executions later within that same day may still be returned.

teamId.description: Restrict the search to a single team within the organization. When omitted, all teams the authenticated user has access to are searched.

errorMessage.description: Substring matched against the execution's error message. Matching snippets are returned in the `highlight` field of each result.

scenarioIds.description: Restrict the search to the specified scenario IDs.

status.description: Filter executions by status. `1` is for success, `2` is for warning, and `3` is for error.

pageSize.description: The maximum number of executions to return. Capped at `500`.

pageIndex.description: The zero-based index of the page to return.

imtId.description: The internal identifier of the execution. Equal to `id` unless an alternate cursor was supplied.

id.description: The execution ID.

kindId.description: The ID of the scenario the execution belongs to.

scenarioId.description: The ID of the scenario the execution belongs to.

scenarioName.description: The name of the scenario at the time of the execution.

timestamp.description: ISO timestamp when the execution started.

type.description: The trigger type of the execution (for example `auto`, `manual`).

instant.description: Whether the scenario was triggered instantly (for example, by a webhook).

authorId.description: The ID of the user who triggered the execution. Null for system-triggered runs.

teamId.description: The ID of the team that owns the scenario.

organizationId.description: The ID of the organization that owns the scenario.

duration.description: The duration of the execution in milliseconds.

operations.description: The number of operations consumed by the execution.

centicredits.description: The number of centicredits consumed by the execution.

transfer.description: The amount of data transferred during the execution, in bytes.

status.description: The execution status. `1` is for success, `2` is for warning, and `3` is for error.

eventType.description: The execution event type stored in the underlying log entry.

errorMessage.description: The error message associated with a failed execution. Null when the execution did not produce an error.

highlight.description: Snippets from the error message that matched the `errorMessage` filter, with the matched substring wrapped in highlight markers. Null when no `errorMessage` filter was applied.

post.description:
Searches the module input/output (IO) data produced by scenario executions across the organization (or a specific team) using a free-text `query`. Matches are grouped by execution: each result contains up to 5 IO-data records that matched the query together with the metadata of the parent execution.

Use the optional filters to restrict the search to specific teams, scenarios, apps, modules, or a time window.

**The `from` and `to` bounds are applied at UTC-day granularity** — only the calendar-day portion of each timestamp is honored. IO-data records earlier or later within the same UTC day as a supplied bound may still appear in the results, and the execution metadata attached to each match may include executions from the immediately adjacent UTC days.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.

query.description: The free-text expression to match against scenario module IO data. Supports quoted phrases.

teamId.description: Restrict the search to a single team within the organization. When omitted, all teams the authenticated user has access to are searched.

from.description:
Lower bound of the time window, as a millisecond Unix timestamp.

Applied at **UTC-day granularity**: the calendar day of this value is the earliest day searched in the IO-data index, but records earlier within that same day may still be returned. Execution metadata joins use this same bound expanded by one day on each side.

to.description:
Upper bound of the time window, as a millisecond Unix timestamp.

Applied at **UTC-day granularity**: the calendar day of this value is the latest day searched in the IO-data index, but records later within that same day may still be returned. Execution metadata joins use this same bound expanded by one day on each side.

scenarioIds.description: Restrict the search to the specified scenario IDs.

appNames.description: Restrict the search to executions that used any of the listed apps (by app name).

moduleNames.description: Restrict the search to executions that used any of the listed module names.

executionIds.description: Restrict the search to the specified execution IDs.

status.description: Which execution outcome's IO data to full-text search. `1` searches successful (`data`) records, `2` searches warning records, and `3` searches error (`err`) records. Multiple values may be combined. Defaults to `[1, 2, 3]` (all outcomes) when omitted.

pageSize.description: The maximum number of execution groups to return. Capped at `500`.

pageIndex.description: The zero-based index of the page to return.

ioData.description: Up to 5 IO-data records from the execution that matched the query. Each record contains the bundle fields produced or consumed by a module, plus the metadata listed below.

kind.description: The kind of the IO record (for example `input`, `output`).

kindId.description: The ID of the scenario the record belongs to.

executionId.description: The ID of the execution the record belongs to.

metadata.description: Free-form metadata captured alongside the IO record.

highlight.description: Snippets from the matched field (`data`, `warning.message`, or `err.message`) that matched `query`, with the matched substring wrapped in `<em>` markers. Empty when nothing matched. Snippets are not remapped to per-bundle paths.

imtId.description: The internal identifier of the execution. Equal to `id` unless an alternate cursor was supplied.

id.description: The execution ID.

kindId.description: The ID of the scenario the execution belongs to.

scenarioId.description: The ID of the scenario the execution belongs to.

scenarioName.description: The name of the scenario at the time of the execution.

timestamp.description: ISO timestamp when the execution started.

type.description: The trigger type of the execution (for example `auto`, `manual`).

instant.description: Whether the scenario was triggered instantly (for example, by a webhook).

authorId.description: The ID of the user who triggered the execution. Null for system-triggered runs.

teamId.description: The ID of the team that owns the scenario.

organizationId.description: The ID of the organization that owns the scenario.

duration.description: The duration of the execution in milliseconds.

operations.description: The number of operations consumed by the execution.

centicredits.description: The number of centicredits consumed by the execution.

transfer.description: The amount of data transferred during the execution, in bytes.

status.description: The execution status. `1` is for success, `2` is for warning, and `3` is for error.

eventType.description: The execution event type stored in the underlying log entry.

errorMessage.description: The error message associated with a failed execution. Null when the execution did not produce an error.

highlight.description: Snippets from the error message that matched the `errorMessage` filter, with the matched substring wrapped in highlight markers. Null when no `errorMessage` filter was applied.

post.summary: Count scenarios and executions matching a full-text query
post.description:
Counts how many scenarios and executions across the organization (or a specific team) produced module input/output (IO) data matching a free-text `query`. Returns global, exact totals plus a per-scenario breakdown of how many distinct executions matched.

Counts are computed over the entire match set (not a page): there is no client-facing pagination. Internally the endpoint pages through all matching `(scenario, execution)` pairs using an Elasticsearch composite aggregation.

**The `from` and `to` bounds are applied at UTC-day granularity** — only the calendar-day portion of each timestamp is honored. When omitted, the search defaults to the last 7 days, mirroring the other organization scenario-log endpoints.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.

query.description: The free-text expression to match against scenario module IO data. Supports quoted phrases.

teamId.description: Restrict the search to a single team within the organization. When omitted, all teams the authenticated user has access to are searched.

from.description:
Lower bound of the time window, as a millisecond Unix timestamp.

Applied at **UTC-day granularity**. When both `from` and `to` are omitted, the last 7 days are searched.

to.description:
Upper bound of the time window, as a millisecond Unix timestamp.

Applied at **UTC-day granularity**.

scenarioIds.description: Restrict the search to the specified scenario IDs.

status.description: Which execution outcome's IO data to full-text search. `1` searches successful (`data`) records, `2` searches warning records, and `3` searches error (`err`) records. Multiple values may be combined. Defaults to `[1, 2, 3]` (all outcomes) when omitted.

totalScenarios.description: The total number of distinct scenarios that produced IO data matching the query.

totalExecutions.description: The total number of distinct executions that produced IO data matching the query.

executionsCount.description: The number of distinct executions of this scenario that matched the query.

post.summary: Count executions by module for a full-text query within a scenario
post.description:
For a single scenario, counts how many distinct executions produced module input/output (IO) data matching a free-text `query`.

**The `from` and `to` bounds are applied at UTC-day granularity** — only the calendar-day portion of each timestamp is honored. When omitted, the search defaults to the last 7 days.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.

query.description: The free-text expression to match against scenario module IO data. Supports quoted phrases.

scenarioId.description: The ID of the scenario to break down by module.

teamId.description: Restrict the search to a single team within the organization. When omitted, all teams the authenticated user has access to are searched.

from.description:
Lower bound of the time window, as a millisecond Unix timestamp.

Applied at **UTC-day granularity**. When both `from` and `to` are omitted, the last 7 days are searched.

to.description:
Upper bound of the time window, as a millisecond Unix timestamp.

Applied at **UTC-day granularity**.

status.description: Which execution outcome's IO data to full-text search. `1` searches successful (`data`) records, `2` searches warning records, and `3` searches error (`err`) records. Multiple values may be combined. Defaults to `[1, 2, 3]` (all outcomes) when omitted.

executionsCount.description: The number of distinct executions of the scenario in which this module produced IO data matching the query.
