get.description:
Returns the private-spaces settings for the organization.

When the organization has no private-spaces settings configured yet, defaults are returned:
- `privateSpacesAutoCreationEnabled` defaults to `false`
- `defaultOperationsLimit` defaults to `1000`
- `addAdminsAsObservers` defaults to `false`

Once configured, the stored value is returned as-is — including an explicit `null`, which means **unlimited** operations (distinct from the never-configured default of `1000`).

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.

privateSpacesAutoCreationEnabled.description: When `true`, a private space is automatically created for each new member of the organization.

defaultOperationsLimit.description: Default operations limit applied when a private space is auto-created. `null` means unlimited. Defaults to `1000` when never configured.

addAdminsAsObservers.description: When `true`, organization admins and the owner are added as `Team Observer` to private spaces. Defaults to `false`.

403.description: Forbidden - Insufficient rights to view organization settings

patch.description:
Updates the organization's private-spaces settings. All body fields are optional — only the keys provided are written.

### Confirmation requirement
The `?confirmed=true` query parameter is required when the request includes any of these bulk operations, otherwise the endpoint responds with `IM004`:
- `privateSpacesAutoCreationEnabled: true` — **bulk-creates a private space for every eligible organization member** (those holding the `personal team own view` permission) that does not already own one.
- `privateSpacesAutoCreationEnabled: false` — **also deletes every existing private-space team in the organization.**
- `addAdminsAsObservers: true` / `false` — **reconciles every existing private-space team**: enabling adds org admins/owner as `Team Observer`; disabling removes those implicit observers.
- `bulkUpdateExistingLimits: true` — overwrites `limits.operations` on every existing private-space team in the organization to match the `defaultOperationsLimit`, and re-evaluates each team's pause state. `defaultOperationsLimit` **must be provided in the same request** when `bulkUpdateExistingLimits` is `true` (a number, or `null` for unlimited), otherwise the request is rejected with `IM005`. This ensures the value applied org-wide is stated explicitly rather than implicitly inherited from the org's currently stored setting.

Changing `defaultOperationsLimit` alone (to any value, including `null` / `0` / lower) only affects future creations and does NOT require confirmation.

### License requirement
Enabling `addAdminsAsObservers` requires the organization to hold the `privateSpacesObservability` license parameter. Without it, the request is rejected with `SC402`.

### Owner-only field
`addAdminsAsObservers` can be changed **only by the organization owner**. Other organization admins may update the remaining settings on this endpoint, but a request that includes `addAdminsAsObservers` (either `true` or `false`) from a non-owner is rejected with `IM002`.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Required when enabling/disabling auto-creation, toggling admin observers, or bulk-updating existing limits.

privateSpacesAutoCreationEnabled.description: When `true`, new members of the organization automatically get a private space.

defaultOperationsLimit.description: Default operations limit applied when a private space is auto-created. Pass `null` to set unlimited operations.

bulkUpdateExistingLimits.description: When `true`, applies `defaultOperationsLimit` to every existing private-space team in the organization. Requires `defaultOperationsLimit` to be provided in the same request (rejected with `IM005` otherwise).

addAdminsAsObservers.description: When `true`, organization admins/owner are added as `Team Observer` to private spaces (and to all existing ones); when `false`, those implicit observers are removed. **Settable by the organization owner only** — a non-owner request including this field is rejected with `IM002`. Enabling requires the `privateSpacesObservability` license (rejected with `SC402` otherwise).

bulkCreatedTeamIds.description: IDs of private-space teams created by enabling auto-creation. Empty unless `privateSpacesAutoCreationEnabled` was set to `true`.

bulkUpdatedTeamIds.description: IDs of private-space teams whose limits were overwritten by the bulk update. Empty unless `bulkUpdateExistingLimits` was set.

bulkDeletedTeamIds.description: IDs of private-space teams that were deleted as part of disabling auto-creation. Empty unless `privateSpacesAutoCreationEnabled` was set to `false`.

observersAddedTeamIds.description: IDs of private-space teams that gained admin `Team Observer` memberships. Empty unless `addAdminsAsObservers` was set to `true`.

observersRemovedTeamIds.description: IDs of private-space teams that had admin `Team Observer` memberships removed. Empty unless `addAdminsAsObservers` was set to `false`.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid body

402.description: Payment required - The organization lacks the `privateSpacesObservability` license needed to enable admin observers

example.message: Adding admins as observers requires the private spaces observability license.

403.description: Forbidden - Insufficient rights to modify organization settings, or a non-owner attempted to change `addAdminsAsObservers`

message.description: Generic, localized message for the error code.

example.detail: Only the organization owner can change addAdminsAsObservers.

406.description: Confirmation required for a bulk change

example.message: Confirmation required to bulk-create, bulk-delete, bulk-update limits, or change admin observers.

post.description:
Clones the specified scenario. The response contains all information about the scenario clone.

You have to know which app integrations the scenario contains. You can get a list of apps used in the scenario with the API call `GET /scenarios/{scenarioId}` in the `usedPackages` array.

If you are cloning the scenario to a different team and the scenario contains an app module, webhook or data store, you have to either:

- map the entity ID to a different entity with the correct properties. For example, you can map an app module connection to a different connection of the same app with the same scopes, or
 - use the `notAnalyze` query parameter to turn off the scenario clone blueprint analysis.

When you turn off the scenario blueprint analysis you can map the entity ID to the `null` value, which omits the entity settings.

The scenario blueprint analysis makes sure that the scenario clone will work without further changes. If you turn off the scenario blueprint analysis, check the configuration of all entities in the scenario clone.

If you are cloning the scenario to a different team and the scenario contains a custom app or a custom function, which is not available for the users in the team, use the `confirmed` query parameter to confirm cloning of the scenario. Otherwise, you get an error listing the custom function that you have to create in the team.

Refer to the request body parameters description and examples for more information.

parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. You can get the `scenarioId` with the [List scenarios](/api-reference/scenarios/get--scenarios.md) API call.
parameters.name: scenarioId

parameters.description: The ID of the organization.
parameters.name: organizationId
cols[]
parameters.description:
Specifies columns that are returned in the response. Use the `cols[]` parameter for every column that you want to return in the response. For example `GET /endpoint?cols[]=key1&cols[]=key2` to get both `key1` and `key2` columns in the response.

[Check the "Filtering" section for a full example.](/api-documentation/pagination-sorting-filtering/filtering)

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: If the scenario contains a custom app or a custom function, that is not available in the team, you have to set the `confirmed` parameter to `true` to clone the scenario. Otherwise you get an error and the scenario is not cloned.

parameters.name: notAnalyze
parameters.description: If you are cloning a scenario to a different team, you have to map the scenario entities (connections, data stores, webhooks, ...) from the original to the clone. If you cannot map all of the scenario entities, set the `notAnalyze` parameter to `true` to suppress the scenario blueprint analysis.

name.description: The name for the scenario clone. The maximum length of the name is 120 characters.

teamId.description: The ID of the team to which you want to clone the scenario.

account.description: Specify pairs of original and clone connection IDs to map connections to the cloned scenario.

key.description: Specify pairs of original and clone key IDs to map keys to the cloned scenario.

hook.description: Specify pairs of original and clone hook IDs to map webhooks to the cloned scenario.

device.description: Specify pairs of original and clone device IDs to map devices to the cloned scenario.

udt.description: Specify pairs of original and clone data structure IDs to map data structures to the cloned scenario.

datastore.description: Specify pairs of original and clone data store IDs to map data stores to the cloned scenario.

states.description: Set to `true` to clone also states of the scenario modules, for example last scenario trigger execution. Setting to `false` resets the state information of the scenario modules in the scenario clone.

cloneScenario.description: Clone the specified scenario to the team with the specified `teamId`.

suppressBlueprintAnalysis.description:
Suppress scenario clone blueprint analysis to clone the scenario without mapping the connections, data stores or other scenario entities. 

If you do not map all of the scenario entities to the scenario clone, you will have to edit the scenario manually.

post.description:
Restores a soft-deleted ("trashed") scenario with the given ID back to active, as long as it
is still within the 30-day recovery window. The scenario's runnable state (apps, connections,
modules, webhook wiring) is re-derived best-effort from its surviving blueprint; if the
blueprint references something deleted while the scenario sat in trash, the restore still
succeeds and the scenario is flagged with `containsErrors` so it can be fixed by editing.
Requires the `scenario edit` permission. A scenario beyond the 30-day window can no longer be
restored.

parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. You can get the `scenarioId` with the [List scenarios](/api-reference/scenarios/get--scenarios.md) API call.
parameters.name: scenarioId

400.description: Scenario trash is not enabled on this environment (`IM903`, returned while the `is_scenarios_trash_enabled` feature flag is off).

403.description: The user does not have the `scenario edit` permission for this scenario.

404.description: The scenario does not exist or is beyond the 30-day restore window.

put.description:
**Why the docs are internal:**
It's not clear how to use this EP. The example suggest that the EP can only change the trigger epoch.
But the EP's SQL routine and original documentation suggest that the EP is supposed to update any module.
I wasn't able to reproduce that use case even with the simplest modules. Furthermore, the EP always returns successful response, even though it didn't update anything.
This causes a lot of confusion and frustration ot the users. So until the usage of the EP is clear the docs should be hidden.
2023-06-06, agreed with Ondrej Vesely.
**Original docs:**
Sets the module data for a scenario with a given ID by passing new values in the request body. It replaces the entire resource with the new values. In the response, it returns the confirmation if the scenario was updated.

parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. You can get the `scenarioId` with the [List scenarios](/api-reference/scenarios/get--scenarios.md) API call.
parameters.name: scenarioId

requestBody.description: The request body must contain the object with module data that strictly depends on the specific module. Keys in the object are IDs of the modules for which data are going to be set. Values are arbitrary objects that are supplied to the modules.

get.description: Verifies whether the module data is set or not. This endpoint doesn't retrieve the module data.

parameters.name: moduleId

post.description:
Activates the specified scenario. Also runs the scenario if the scenario is scheduled to run at regular intervals. Read more about [scenario scheduling](https://www.make.com/en/help/scenarios/scheduling-a-scenario).

The API call response contains the scenario ID and the scenario `isActive` property set to `true`.

parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. You can get the `scenarioId` with the [List scenarios](/api-reference/scenarios/get--scenarios.md) API call.
parameters.name: scenarioId

post.description: Deactivates and stops the specified scenario if the scenario is running. The API call response contains the scenario ID and the scenario `isActive` property set to `false`.

parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. You can get the `scenarioId` with the [List scenarios](/api-reference/scenarios/get--scenarios.md) API call.
parameters.name: scenarioId

post.description:
Runs the specified scenario. The scenario has to be active. If your scenario has required scenario inputs you have to provide the scenario inputs in the request body. If the scenario provides scenario outputs, these are returned in the response.

**Note:** [Organization request limits](https://developers.make.com/api-documentation/getting-started/rate-limiting) do **not** apply for this endpoint.

parameters.name: scenarioId
parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. Get the ID of the scenario with the API call `GET /scenarios`.

data.description: If your scenario has inputs specify the input parameters and values in the `data` object.

responsive.description:
If set to `true` the Make API waits until the scenario finishes. The response contains the scenario `status` and `executionId`. If the scenario execution takes longer than 40 seconds, the API call returns the time out error, but the scenario is still executed.

If set to `false` the API call returns immediately without waiting. The response contains only the `executionId`.

status.description:
Status of the scenario execution:

- `1`: success
 - `2`: warning
 - `3`: error.

responsiveWithin40s.summary: Responsive run that finished within 40 seconds

responsiveErrorWithin40s.summary: Responsive unsuccessful run that finished within 40 seconds

error.message: An error occurred while executing the scenario.
error.name: RuntimeError

responsiveAfter40s.summary: Responsive run taking longer than 40 seconds

post.description: Replays the specified scenario execution. The scenario has to be active.

parameters.name: scenarioId
parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. Get the ID of the scenario with the API call `GET /scenarios`.

executionIds.description: An array of executionIds. Currently only the first one will be replayed.

get.description: Returns a list of replayable executions for a given scenario

parameters.name: scenarioId
parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. Get the ID of the scenario with the API call `GET /scenarios`.

post.description:
This endpoint is set to internal because the scenario drafts feature is available only on the Celonis platform. In Make, this endpoint has no functionality.
Original description:
Publishes a draft of a scenario with a given ID. In the response, it returns all details of the published scenario.

parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. You can get the `scenarioId` with the [List scenarios](/api-reference/scenarios/get--scenarios.md) API call.
parameters.name: scenarioId

get.description: Retrieves the scenario inputs and outputs specification for the specified scenario. Check out the [scenario inputs and outputs documentation](https://help.make.com/scenario-inputs-and-outputs) in the Make help center.The scenario inputs and outputs feature is available with all plans. [Read more about Make pricing.](https://www.make.com/en/pricing)

parameters.name: scenarioId
parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. Get the list of scenarios with the API call `GET /scenarios`.

patch.description:
Updates specification of the scenario inputs. Check out the [scenario inputs documentation](https://www.make.com/en/help/scenarios/scenario-inputs) in the Make help center.

If you want to enable the scenario inputs you have to set the scenario scheduling to "On demand" first, otherwise you get error 422 (IM016). You can use the API call:

`PATCH /scenarios/{scenarioId}?confirmed=true`

with the request body:

`{"scheduling": "{\"type\":\"on-demand\"}"}`

You can disable inputs for the specified scenario by sending a payload with an empty `input` array.

The response contains the updated scenario inputs specification.

The scenario inputs feature requires your account to have the pricing plan **Pro** or higher. [Read more about Make pricing.](https://www.make.com/en/pricing)

parameters.name: scenarioId
parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. Get the list of scenarios with the API call `GET /scenarios`.

interface.description: Contains the `input` array with specification of the scenario input parameters.

interface-update.description: Updates scenario inputs to the specification you provide in the request body.

delete-interface.description: Disable scenario inputs for the specified scenario by sending empty `input` and `output` arrays in the request body.

get.description:
Retrieves a list of daily centicredits, operations, and data transfer usage for a specified scenario over the past 30 days.

By default, the endpoint uses the timezone of the user making the API call to define the start and end of each day in the 30-day timeframe.

To use the organization's timezone instead, set the `organizationTimezone` parameter to `true`. This ensures that the daily aggregates align with the organization's operational hours. This is especially useful for scenarios where aggregated data needs to align with the organization's operational hours.

For instance, a remote data analyst in India working for a Czech company can set `organizationTimezone=true` to ensure the usage data reflects the company's timezone, providing more relevant and accurate insights for organizational reporting and analysis.

For more information on timezones in Make, please refer to our [Help Center article](https://www.make.com/en/help/general/manage-time-zones).

parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. You can get the `scenarioId` with the [List scenarios](/api-reference/scenarios/get--scenarios.md) API call.
parameters.name: scenarioId
organizationTimezone
parameters.description: When set to `true`, the endpoint will calculate and return usage data based on the organization's timezone instead of the user's local timezone.

teamId

blueprint.description: The blueprint of the scenario recovery snapshot

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the scenario recovery snapshot was created

status.description: Status indicating no scenario recovery snapshot exists

put.description: Saves a scenario recovery snapshot for a given scenario and removes the present one if it exists. scenarioId = 0 is a sentinel for unsaved draft scenarios (no ownership check required). Uses team-scoped storage for cross-team draft isolation.

delete.description: Discards a scenario recovery snapshot for a given scenario. scenarioId = 0 is a sentinel for unsaved draft scenarios (no ownership check required). Deletes the team-scoped recovery key.

get.description:
Returns completed execution summaries across all scenarios visible to the API token,
ordered ascending by completion time. Designed for polling integrations that need a
single feed of "what completed since last poll" across the full scenario fleet.

**Filtering:** Only `EXECUTION_END` records are returned (in-progress executions excluded).

**Pagination:** Use `since` as the cursor — store `max(timestamp)` from the last response
and pass it as `since` on the next poll.

**Limit:** `since` must not be older than 90 days.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description:
ISO8601 UTC timestamp. The server returns only executions whose completion time is
**strictly greater than** this value. Store `max(timestamp)` from the last response
as the next `since` cursor.

parameters.name: until
parameters.description:
ISO8601 UTC timestamp upper bound (inclusive). Defaults to the current server time
when omitted.

parameters.name: pg[limit]
parameters.description: Maximum number of execution summaries to return. Capped at 200.

imtId.description: Opaque cursor combining epoch ms and execution id.

id.description: Execution ID (32-char hex).

400.description: Invalid query parameters (e.g. `since` missing or older than 90 days).

403.description: Token lacks `scenarios:read` scope.

get.description: Retrieves a collection of all logs for a scenario with a given ID. Returned logs are sorted by `imtId` in descending order.

parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. You can get the `scenarioId` with the [List scenarios](/api-reference/scenarios/get--scenarios.md) API call.
parameters.name: scenarioId
from
parameters.description: The timestamp in milliseconds that defines the starting point of time from which the logs should be retrieved. Older logs will not be returned.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: The timestamp in milliseconds that defines the ending point of time to which the logs should be retrieved. Newer logs will not be returned.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filters logs by the execution status. `1` is for success, `2` is for warning, and `3` is for error.

parameters.name: durationFrom
parameters.description: Filters logs to only include executions with a duration greater than or equal to this value (in milliseconds).

parameters.name: durationTo
parameters.description: Filters logs to only include executions with a duration less than or equal to this value (in milliseconds).

parameters.name: operationsFrom
parameters.description: Filters logs to only include executions with an operations count greater than or equal to this value.

parameters.name: operationsTo
parameters.description: Filters logs to only include executions with an operations count less than or equal to this value.

parameters.name: transferFrom
parameters.description: Filters logs to only include executions with a data transfer greater than or equal to this value (in bytes).

parameters.name: transferTo
parameters.description: Filters logs to only include executions with a data transfer less than or equal to this value (in bytes).

parameters.name: executionName
parameters.description: Filters logs by the execution name (partial match).

parameters.name: creditsFrom
parameters.description: Filters logs to only include executions with credits greater than or equal to this value (in centicredits).

parameters.name: creditsTo
parameters.description: Filters logs to only include executions with credits less than or equal to this value (in centicredits).

parameters.name: showCheckRuns
parameters.description: If set to `true`, check runs are included in the returned results. By default they are excluded. Check runs concern scenarios starting with a trigger in cases when the trigger does not find anything new.

parameters.name: pg[offset]
parameters.description: The number of entities you want to skip before getting entities you want.

parameters.name: pg[limit]
parameters.description: The maximum number of entities you want to get in the response.

parameters.name: pg[showLast]
parameters.description: Include records with `last` value in the result set. Just in case of the `last` based paging.

parameters.name: pg[last]
parameters.description: The last retrieved key. In response, you get only entries that follow after the key.

parameters.name: pg[sortBy]
parameters.description: The value that will be used to sort returned entities by.

parameters.name: pg[sortDir]
parameters.description: The sorting order. It accepts the ascending and descending direction specifiers.

authorName.description: Name of the user who triggered the scenario run. Null when authorId is missing or unknown.

last.description: Shown only when using the `last` based pagination.

showLast.description: Shown only when using the `last` based pagination.

offset.description: Shown only when using the `offset` based pagination.

scheduled.description: Returns log of the scheduled scenario

manual.description: Returns log of the manual scenario

get.description: Retrieves an execution log with a given ID for a scenario with a given ID. It returns the execution details such as execution duration, type, and status.

parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. You can get the `scenarioId` with the [List scenarios](/api-reference/scenarios/get--scenarios.md) API call.
parameters.name: scenarioId

parameters.description: The unique ID of the scenario execution. It can be retrieved from the [List scenario logs](/api-reference/scenarios/logs/get--scenarios--scenarioid--logs.md) endpoint under the ID key.
parameters.name: executionId

authorName.description: Name of the user who triggered the scenario run. Null when authorId is missing or unknown.

get.description:
Retrieves enriched execution detail for a given execution. Without `include` the response
shape is identical to `GET /scenarios/{scenarioId}/logs/{executionId}`. Use the `include`
query parameter to request additional data sections in a single call.

parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. You can get the `scenarioId` with the [List scenarios](/api-reference/scenarios/get--scenarios.md) API call.
parameters.name: scenarioId

parameters.description: The unique ID of the scenario execution. It can be retrieved from the [List scenario logs](/api-reference/scenarios/logs/get--scenarios--scenarioid--logs.md) endpoint under the ID key.
parameters.name: executionId
include
parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of optional data sections to include in the response.
Supported values: `blueprint`, `events`, `bundles`. Unknown values are silently ignored.

id.description: Execution ID

bundles.description:
Present when `include=bundles`. Input/output bundles keyed by module id;
each value is an array of records. Non-agent modules emit one record per
stored bundle; AI-agent modules collapse a cycle's input + output into one
record with an `agent` object (structural-only — message bodies omitted).
Each `data[]` element is a stringified bundle capped at 256 KB; oversized
elements are sliced and suffixed with ` ... `, so the snippet may not be
valid JSON.

truncated.description: True when the 256 KB per-element cap shortened any data[] element of this bundle.

get.description: Retrieves details about an execution.

get.description: Retrieves a collection of all blueprints versions for a scenario with a given ID. Due to the regular archiving process, only the versions that are not older than 60 days can be retrieved. Each returned blueprint version consists of the date and time of the blueprint creation, IDs of the blueprint version and related scenario, and the information if the blueprint was created for the draft or live scenario version.

get.description: Retrieves buildtime variables of a scenario with the given ID. Buildtime variables could be team or user defined, team defined ones are prefixed with a `TAC_` and user defined variables are prefixed with a `PAC_`. `TAC_`s can be used within the scenario as per its input spec by the entire team, whereas `PAC_`s can only be used within the scenario by the user who added them.

parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. You can get the `scenarioId` with the [List scenarios](/api-reference/scenarios/get--scenarios.md) API call.
parameters.name: scenarioId

post.summary: Add new buildtime variables to scenario metadata
post.description: Adds new buildtime team or user defined variable/s. Buildtime variables should be prefixed either with a `TAC_` (for team defined variables) or with a `PAC_` (for personal user defined variables), followed by the connection value. If a variable already exists, an error will be thrown. If a variable's name is not within scenario input specification, an error will be thrown. If the adding of new variables was successful the reponse would be `OK`.

parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. You can get the `scenarioId` with the [List scenarios](/api-reference/scenarios/get--scenarios.md) API call.
parameters.name: scenarioId

name.description: The name for the buildtime variable. The maximum length of the name is 128 characters.

value.description: The value for the buildtime variable. It should start either with TAC_ or PAC_ followed by numeric chars only.

put.summary: Update buildtime variables in scenario metadata
put.description: Updates team or user defined buildtime variable/s. The endpoint updates and overwrites exsiting records with the newly provided values, meaning any existing buildtime variable which is not provided through the payload will be overwritten. Buildtime variables should be prefixed either with a `TAC_` (for team defined variables) or with a `PAC_` (for personal user defined variables), followed by the connection value. If a variable doesn't exist, it will be added provided that its name is within the scenario input specification. If the updating of variables was successful the reponse would be `OK`.

parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. You can get the `scenarioId` with the [List scenarios](/api-reference/scenarios/get--scenarios.md) API call.
parameters.name: scenarioId

name.description: The name for the buildtime variable. The maximum length of the name is 128 characters.

value.description: The value for the buildtime variable. It should start either with TAC_ or PAC_ followed by numeric chars only.

delete.description: Deletes a buildtime variable with a given value for a scenario with a given ID and returns `OK` in the response.

parameters.description: The ID of the scenario. You can get the `scenarioId` with the [List scenarios](/api-reference/scenarios/get--scenarios.md) API call.

value

get.description:
Retrieves a list of scenarios and their current consumption. Make counts the number of consumed centicredits, operations, and transferred data according to your reset period. If you have a monthly reset period, the response contains a list of scenarios that consumed at least one hundred centicredits (one operation) in the current reset period with their consumption and data transfer..

If you have a yearly reset period, the response contains a list of scenarios that consumed at least one hundred centicredits (one operation) in the last 60 days, or from the last reset if that period is shorter than 60 days.

60 days after the reset the scenario consumptions turn into running totals over the last 60 days.

For example, let's assume that you have a set of scenarios that consume 100000 centicredits (1000 operations) every day. On the 61st day from your last reset, you turn off a scenario that consumes 20000 centicredits (200 operations) every day, reducing your consumption to 80000 centicredits (800 operations) per day.

If you would have the yearly reset period and you would be tracking your scenario consumptions, you would get the following data:

Day Consumptions Delta
1 1000
2 2000 1000
3 3000 1000
...
60 60000 1000
61 59800 -200
62 59600 -200

The negative difference between scenario consumptions on the 60th and 61st day happens because on the 61st day, the endpoint doesn't collect data from the first day anymore. Your scenario consumptions contain a total over the time period from the second day until the 61st day.

For billing, Make uses a different system which ensures accurate billing.

Check the parameter `lastReset` in the response for the timestamp of the start of your current reset period. You can also view the end of your current restart period in your dashboard in the **Usage reset** field. Set the `organizationId` or `teamId` parameters to limit the results to a specific organization or team.

parameters.description: The ID of the team.

get.description: Gets custom properties data of the specified scenario.

parameters.name: scenarioId
parameters.description: The ID of the scenario.

customProperties.description: This scenario handles notifications for the Product team in London.

post.description:
Fills in custom properties data for the specified scenario. The scenario has to have no custom properties data.

The data you fill in has to conform to the current custom scenario properties structure. You have to specify a value for every required item, otherwise you get an error.

To update existing data, use the following API calls:

- [Update custom properties data](./patch--scenarios--scenarioid--custom-properties.md)
- [Set custom properties data](./put--scenarios--scenarioid--custom-properties.md)

parameters.name: scenarioId
parameters.description: The ID of the scenario.

example.description: Scenario for the Marketing team to share information with other stakeholders.

customProperties.description: Scenario for the Marketing team to share information with other stakeholders.

patch.description:
Updates custom properties data of the specified scenario. Make updates only the custom properties data you specify in the request body.

The custom property has to contain data already. The update data have to conform to the current custom scenario properties structure. If the initial value is empty, then the API call returns an error.

To fill in first values to the custom properties, use the API call to [fill in custom properties data](./post--scenarios--scenarioid--custom-properties.md).

parameters.name: scenarioId
parameters.description: The ID of the scenario.

basic-example.description: Update custom property item data. Make updates only the data you specify in the request body.

options-example.description: Update custom property item data from a list of options. If you specify a value that is not part of the list of available options, you get an error.

customProperties.description: This scenario is handling notifications for the Product team in London.
This scenario is handling notifications for the Product team in London.

put.description:
Sets custom properties data for the specified scenario. Make replaces the original data with the data you send in the request body.

You have to specify a value for every required custom properties structure item, otherwise you get an error.

The scenario has to have custom property data already. The new data set has to conform to the current custom scenario properties structure. If the initial data is empty, then the API call returns an error.

To fill in first values to the custom properties, use the API call to [fill in custom properties data](./post--scenarios--scenarioid--custom-properties.md).

parameters.name: scenarioId
parameters.description: The ID of the scenario.

example.description: Scenario for the London engineering team

customProperties.description: Scenario for the London engineering team

delete.description:
Deletes custom properties data.
Deleting custom properties data is irreversible.
Use the `confirmed` parameter to confirm deleting the data, otherwise you get an error and the data are not deleted.

parameters.name: scenarioId
parameters.description: The ID of the scenario.

post.description: Assigns a team label to a single scenario. A team label may only be applied to scenarios in that same team. Idempotent.

403.description: Insufficient rights — the user lacks scenario-edit permission, the scenario does not exist or is not accessible, or the label belongs to a different team than the scenario.

delete.description: Removes a team label from a single scenario. Idempotent.

403.description: Insufficient rights — the user lacks scenario-edit permission, the scenario does not exist or is not accessible, or the label belongs to a different team than the scenario.

get.description: Retrieves a collection of all apps available to the authenticated user.

parameters.name: opensource
parameters.description: If set to `true`, this parameter returns apps available to all users. If set to `false`, it retrieves the apps available to the authenticated user.

parameters.name: cols[]
parameters.description: Specifies the group of values to return. For example, you may want to retrieve only the names of the available apps.

post.description: Creates a new app with data passed in the request body. In the response, it returns all details of the created app.

parameters.name: cols[]
parameters.description: Specifies the group of values to return.

app.name: postman-test-app-1
app.label: Postman Test App
app.description: This is a testing app from Postman

app.name: postman-test-app-1
app.label: Postman Test App
app.description: This is a testing app from Postman

post.description: Clones the app to the next major version with the same app name. Requires the "allow_apps" feature flag to be enabled.

get.description: Get app client id and client secret.

parameters.name: SDK_appName
parameters.description: The numeric version of the app.

parameters.name: SDK_appVersion
parameters.description: The numeric version of the app.

put.description: Sets the common data for the app based on the parameters passed in the request body. In the response, it returns all details of common data. Common data usually contain sensitive information like API keys or API secrets and these details are shared across all modules.

schema.description: The JSON object containing the common data.

parameters.name: SDK_appName
parameters.description: The name of the app.

parameters.name: SDK_appVersion
parameters.description: The app version.

post.description: Set app base configuration.

url.description: Request URL

baseUrl.description: Base URL for endpoints starting with /

encodeUrl.description: This directive controls the encoding of URLs. It is on by default, so if you have any special characters in your URL, they will be automatically encoded. But there might be situations where you don't want your URL to be encoded automatically, or you want to control what parts of the URL are encoded. To do this, set this flag to false.

method.description: This directive specifies the HTTP method that will be used to issue the request.

headers.description: This directive specifies headers that will be sent with the request.

qs.description: This directive specifies the query string to use when making the request.

body.description: This directive specifies the request body.

temp.description: The temp directive specifies an object, which can be used to create custom temporary variables. It also creates a temp variable in IML, through which you then access your variables. The temp collection is not persisted and will be lost after the module is done executing.

condition.description: This directive specifies whether to execute the request or not.
Specifies if to execute the request or not.

default.description: Specifies the module output when the condition is false.

aws.description: Helper directive, that will simplify generating AWS signatures.

session.description: AWS session. Note that this only works for services that require session as part of the canonical string.

bucket.description: AWS bucket, unless you're specifying your bucket as part of the path, or the request doesn't use a bucket.

gzip.description: Add an Accept-Encoding header to request compressed content encodings from the server (if not already present) and decode supported content encodings in the response.

followRedirects.description: This directive specifies whether to follow GET HTTP 3xx responses as redirects or never.

followAllRedirects.description: This directive specifies whether to follow non-GET HTTP 3xx responses as redirects or never.

log.description: This directive specifies logging options for both the request and the response.

sanitize.description: An array of paths to sanitize when logging request or response.

oauth.description: Helper directive, that will simplify generating an OAuth1 Authorization headers.

private_key.description: Instead of consumer_secret you can specify a private_key string in PEM format.

token.description: An expression that parses the oauth_token string.

token_secret.description: An expression that parses the oauth_token_secret string.

verifier.description: An expression that parses the oauth_verifier string.

signature_method.description: Specifies the desired method to use when calculating the signature.

transport_method.description: Specifies how OAuth parameters are sent: via query params, header or in a POST body.

body_hash.description: To use Request Body Hash, you can either manually generate it, or you can set this directive to true and the body hash will be generated automatically.

pagination.description: Directive to specify how to process paginated responses.

mergeWithParent.description: This directive specifies if to merge pagination parameters with the original request parameters, or not.

url.description: This directive specifies the URL that will be called when the pagination request is executed.

method.description: This directive specifies the HTTP method to be used when executing the pagination request.

headers.description: This directive specifies the request headers to be used when executing the pagination request.

qs.description: This directive specifies the request query string parameters to be used when executing the pagination request.

body.description: This directive specifies the request body when the request method is anything but GET to be used when executing the pagination request.

condition.description: This directive specifies whether to execute the pagination request or not.

anyOf.description: The error directive specifies the error type and the error message to show the user.

message.description: An expression that parses an error message from response body.

anyOf.description: Error object. You can specify custom errors for different status codes by using the status code as the key (e.g., "404", "500").

post.description: beta, stable

get.summary: All verified and custom apps returned with theme color.

parameters.name: names
parameters.description:
Names of apps to return divided by commas - for example ?names=test-app-6astv7,custom-app-0b5p1w. 
The names parameter may contain maximum 500 values.

name.description: app name

label.description: app label (title)

theme.description: hex code of color theme for app

isCompiled.description:
Indicates whether the app is compiled (true) or not (false).
This is relevant for custom apps that are not compiled yet.

actions.name: makeAnAPICall
actions.label: Make an API Call
actions.description: Performs an arbitrary authorized API request.

triggers.name: watchObjects
triggers.label: Watch Objects
triggers.description: Triggers when a new Object is created.

triggers.name: watchNotifications
triggers.label: Watch Notifications
triggers.description: Triggers when a new Notification is received.

typeId.description:
Module type id. Allowed values:
 - 1 = TRIGGER (Trigger - polling) Use if you wish to watch for any changes in your app/service. Examples are Watch a New Book, which will be triggered whenever a new book has been added to the library.
 - 4 = ACTION Use if the API endpoint returns a single response. Examples are Create a book, Delete a book or Get a Book.
 - 9 = SEARCH Use if the API endpoint returns multiple items. An example is List Books that will find specific books according to search criteria.
 - 10 = CONVERGER (Instant Trigger / webhook) Use if the API endpoint has a webhook available (dedicated or shared). Example is Watch a New Event.
 - 11 = HITL (Responder) Use if you need to send a processed data back to a webhook.
 - 12 = RETURNER (Universal) Use if you want to enable users to perform an arbitrary API call to the service. Examples are Make an API Call and Execute a GraphQL Query.

moduleInitMode.description:
Module init mode:
 * `blank` -  Creates a new blank module (code is empty).
 * `example` - Creates a module from a `model` app (which contains the example codes).
 * `module` - Creates module from existing user's module.

moduleInitSource.description: Required when `moduleInitMode` is `module`. Specifies the name of the source module to clone.

connection.description: The name of the connection to use.

webhook.description: The name of the webhook to use.

post.description: Creates a new endpoint on the app version. The endpoint starts private and unapproved. By default (`endpointInitMode: example`) its sections (`api`, `inputParameters`, `outputParameters`) and `context` are seeded from the built-in endpoint template; pass `endpointInitMode: blank` to start with empty sections instead. All sections can be edited afterwards via the section endpoints. The `name` must be alphanumeric, start with a letter, be 3 to 128 characters long, unique within the app version, and not a reserved keyword. `attachedAccounts` is optional; every connection it lists must belong to the app, otherwise the request is rejected.

get.summary: List the current user's pinned apps for an organization
get.description: Returns every app the current user has any pin under in the specified organization — pinned apps and apps that only have pinned modules. Each entry carries an `isAppPinned` flag: when `true` the app itself is pinned and shows up in the "My apps" tab; when `false` only modules are pinned and the entry is rendered inside the app-detail panel. App and module pins are independent — pinning a module does not pin its app, and unpinning an app leaves its module pins intact (and they reappear if the app is re-pinned). Pins are scoped per (user, organization) and are version-agnostic — the UI resolves the latest available version of each pinned app/module from the catalog. App names follow the convention used by `GET /imt/apps`: SDK apps are surfaced as `app#<name>`, native apps as the bare package name.

appName.description: Name of the pinned app. SDK apps are prefixed with `app#`; native apps are bare package names.

isAppPinned.description: `true` when the app itself is pinned (it appears in the "My apps" tab). `false` when only modules under this app are pinned — the entry is still returned so the UI can render the pinned modules in the app-detail panel.

pinnedModules.description: Pinned modules under this app, ordered oldest-pinned-first.

moduleName.description: Module name within the parent app (matches the `name` field in the app's module manifest).

post.summary: Pin an app for the current user in an organization
post.description: Pins an app for the authenticated user within the specified organization. Accepts both SDK apps (`appName` prefixed with `app#`) and native apps (bare package name). Pins are version-agnostic — the UI always renders the latest available version. Pinning is idempotent — pinning an already-pinned app is a no-op (no error). The app must exist in the catalog; otherwise a 404 is returned.

organizationId.description: Organization to scope the pin to. The current user must be a member.

appName.description: App name. SDK apps are prefixed with `app#`; native apps are bare package names.

delete.summary: Unpin an app for the current user in an organization
delete.description: Removes the user's app pin in the specified organization. Does NOT delete the user's module pins under this app — those rows survive so re-pinning the app restores them. Idempotent — unpinning a non-pinned app is a no-op.

parameters.name: appName
parameters.description: App name. SDK apps are prefixed with `app#`; native apps are bare package names.

post.summary: Pin a module for the current user in an organization
post.description: Pins a module under the specified `appName` for the current user in the specified organization. Pins are version-agnostic — the UI always renders the latest available version. The module must belong to the app; otherwise a 404 is returned. Idempotent — pinning an already-pinned module is a no-op.

parameters.name: appName
parameters.description: App name. SDK apps are prefixed with `app#`; native apps are bare package names.

organizationId.description: Organization to scope the pin to. The current user must be a member.

moduleName.description: Module name within the parent app (matches the `name` field in the app's module manifest).

404.description: Module not found for this app

delete.summary: Unpin a module for the current user in an organization
delete.description: Removes the user's module pin in the specified organization. Does not affect the parent app pin. Idempotent — unpinning a non-pinned module is a no-op.

parameters.name: appName
parameters.description: App name. SDK apps are prefixed with `app#`; native apps are bare package names.

get.description: Gets the list of teams in the organization with specified `organizationId`.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.

parameters.name: includePrivateSpaces
parameters.description:
When `true`, the caller's private space(s) — companies of type `personal` — are included
in the list alongside standard teams. Defaults to `false`. Has no effect when private
spaces are not enabled for the organization. Request `cols[]=type` to tell private spaces
apart from standard teams in the response.

parameters.name: cols[]
parameters.description:
Specifies columns that are returned in the response. Use the `cols[]` parameter for every column that you want to return in the response. For example `GET /endpoint?cols[]=key1&cols[]=key2` to get both `key1` and `key2` columns in the response.

[Check the "Filtering" section for a full example.](/api-documentation/pagination-sorting-filtering/filtering)

parameters.name: pg[limit]
parameters.description: Sets the maximum number of results per page in the API call response. For example, `pg[limit]=100`. The default number varies with different API endpoints.

Distinguishes a standard team from a private space (`personal`). Only returned when requested via

get.description:
Retrieves the collection of team variables. The response contains all team variables if your account has the custom variables feature available. Otherwise, the response contains only Make system variables.

Check availability of the custom variables feature with the API call `GET /organizations/{organizationId}` for the organization to which the team belongs. If the response contains `"customVariables": true` pair in the `license` object then you have access to the custom variables feature.

Refer to the [Make pricing page](https://www.make.com/en/help/general/pricing-parameters) for Make pricing plans overview.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The ID of the team.

post.description:
Creates new team variable. You can check the availability of the custom variables feature with the API call `GET /organizations/{organizationId}` for the organization to which the team belongs. If the API call response contains `"customVariables": true` pair in the `license` object then you have access to the custom variables feature.

A successful response contains all information about the new variable. If you don't have the custom variables feature available then the API call returns the error 404.

Refer to the [Make pricing page](https://www.make.com/en/help/general/pricing-parameters) for Make pricing plans overview.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The ID of the team.

typeId.description:
Number representing the type of the custom variable. The mapping of `typeId` and variable types is as follows:

- 1: `number`,
 - 2: `string`,
 - 3: `boolean`,
 - 4: `date`in ISO 8601 compliant format `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ`. For example: `1998-03-06T12:31:00.000Z`.

value.description: Value assigned to the custom variable.

name.description: The name of the variable. You can use letters, digits, `$` and `_` characters in the custom variable name.

patch.description:
Updates custom team variable with the specified variable `name`. Only parameters specified in the request body are updated.

You can check the availability of the custom variables feature with the API call `GET /organizations/{organizationId}` for the organization to which the team belongs. If the response contains `"customVariables": true` pair in the `license` object then you have access to the custom variables feature.

A successful response contains all information about the updated variable. If you don't have the custom variables feature available then the API call returns the error 404.

Refer to the [Make pricing page](https://www.make.com/en/help/general/pricing-parameters) for the Make pricing plans overview.

Update the variable `typeId` accordingly when you are updating the variable `value`. Make checks whether the variable type and value match in the request body. There is no check for incorrect variable type when you update only variable value and _vice versa_.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The ID of the team.

parameters.name: variableName
parameters.description: The name of the custom variable.

typeId.description:
Number representing the type of the custom variable. The mapping of `typeId` and variable types is as follows:

- 1: `number`,
 - 2: `string`,
 - 3: `boolean`,
 - 4: `date`in ISO 8601 compliant format `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ`. For example: `1998-03-06T12:31:00.000Z`.

value.description: Value assigned to the custom variable.

delete.description:
Deletes team variable.

If you don't have the custom variables feature available then the API call returns error 404. Check the availability of the custom variables feature with the API call `GET /organizations/{organizationId}` for the organization in which the team belongs. If the response contains `"customVariables": true` pair in the `license` object then you have access to the custom variables feature.

Refer to the [Make pricing page](https://www.make.com/en/help/general/pricing-parameters) for Make pricing plans overview.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The ID of the team.

parameters.name: variableName
parameters.description: The name of the custom variable.

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Set to `true` to confirm deleting the custom variable. Otherwise the API call fails with the error IM004 (406).

get.description:
Gets the history of updates of the specified custom variable. The response contains the ID value of the variable history entry and a `diffObject`. The `diffObject` contains:

- the original value,
 - the new value,
 - the timestamp of the update,
 - the author of the update.

If the variable doesn't have any updates, the `diffObject` contains the current value of the variable instead of the original and new values.

The update history entries are sorted from newest to latest.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The ID of the team.

parameters.name: variableName
parameters.description: The name of the custom variable.

action.description: organization_variable_created or organization_variable_edited or team_variable_created or team_variable_edited

post.summary: Check if email is part of team
post.description:
Checks if every given e-mail is an active member of the team. Evaluates to false if any of the input e-mails are not part of the team. This endpoint uses JWT authorization.
Please note that JWT authorization is not available in our open API, use tools like Postman for testing.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The ID of the team.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: JWT token used for internal Authorization.

get.description:
Retrieves a list of daily centicredits, operations, and data transfer usage for all scenarios within a specified team over the past 30 days.

By default, the endpoint uses the timezone of the user making the API call to define the start and end of each day in the 30-day timeframe.

To use the organization's timezone instead, set the `organizationTimezone` parameter to `true`. This ensures that the daily aggregates align with the organization's operational hours. This is especially useful for scenarios where aggregated data needs to align with the organization's operational hours.

For instance, a remote data analyst in India working for a Czech company can set `organizationTimezone=true` to ensure the usage data reflects the company's timezone, providing more relevant and accurate insights for organizational reporting and analysis.

For more information on timezones in Make, please refer to our [Help Center article](https://www.make.com/en/help/general/manage-time-zones).

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The ID of the team.

parameters.name: organizationTimezone
parameters.description: When set to `true`, the endpoint will calculate and return usage data based on the organization's timezone instead of the user's local timezone.

tfaStatus.description:
Two-factor authentication status:
- 0: No TFA set
- 1: TFA active (authenticator app)
- 2: External authentication (Social SSO)

It is only supported on Make's public cloud instances.

supportEligible.description: Whether the user is eligible for contact support. False if all user's organizations are on Free plan and account is older than 90 days.

userTeamIds.description: IDs of teams the user belongs to within the organization. Only returned when explicitly requested via cols[]=userTeamIds and organizationId is provided.

privateSpace.description: The user's personal private space in the organization. Only returned when explicitly requested via cols[]=privateSpace with organizationId on a public instance.

default-cols.description: Get information about the currently authenticated user.

all-cols.description: Get all available information about the currently authenticated user.

get.description: Returns current authorization information for the authenticated user including scope and authentication method used.

scope.description: The scopes for the authenticated user

authUsed.description: The authentication method that was used

default.description: Get current authorization information for the authenticated user.

get.description: Returns whether the authenticated user has a pending onboarding survey. If the survey has been submitted, the response payload is included.

pending.description: Whether the user still needs to complete the onboarding survey.

response.description: The submitted survey response. Only present when `pending` is `false` and a response exists.

pending.summary: User has not completed the survey

no-survey.summary: User never had a survey (legacy or already completed)

post.description: Submits the onboarding survey response for the authenticated user. Optionally pins preferred apps for the user in their organization.

survey_type.description: The type of survey being submitted (e.g., `self_serve`, `enterprise`).

schema_version.description: Version of the survey schema. Allows consumers to interpret the answers.

answers.description: The user's answers to the survey questions, keyed by question name.

preferred_apps.description: Optional list of native app package names to pin for the user. Apps that do not exist are reported as failed without blocking the submission.

team_invite.description: Optional team members to invite during onboarding. Invites are best-effort and do not block the submission.

emails.description: Email addresses to invite as regular organization members.

sso.description: Email addresses to invite via the organization's SSO provider.

200.description: Survey submitted successfully. Side effects (app pinning) are reported individually.

success.description: Whether the core survey submission succeeded.

result.description: The SWARM response from HQ confirming the submission.

pinnedApps.description: Result of each app pin attempt. Empty array if no `preferred_apps` were provided.

error.description: Error message if the pin failed. Only present when `success` is `false`.

invitedUsers.description: Result of each team-invite attempt. Empty array if no `team_invite` was provided. Invites are best-effort, so per-invitee failures do not fail the submission.

error.description: Error reason if the invite failed. Only present when `success` is `false`.

success-with-pins.summary: Survey submitted with all apps pinned successfully

partial-pin-failure.summary: Survey submitted but one app failed to pin

with-team-invites.summary: Survey submitted with team members invited

400.description: Validation error — missing required fields (`survey_type`, `schema_version`, or `answers`).

get.description: Retrieves organization invitations of the currently authenticated user.
Gets the API tokens of the currently authenticated user.

post.description: Create a new API token for the currently authenticated user. Specify the API token scopes in the `scope` array parameter.

label.description: The API token label visible in the Make user profile.

scope.description:
The API scopes provided to the API token. The API scopes determine the scope of operations that you can do with the API token.

Check the list of all existing Make API scopes with the API call `GET /enums/user-api-tokes-scopes`.

get.description: Gets the OAuth connections for the currently authenticated user.

delete.description: Delete a specific OAuth connection for the currently authenticated user.

Deletes currently authenticated user's API token with the specified creation timestamp. Get the API token creation timestamp with the API call `GET /users/me/api-tokens` in the parameter value `created`. Copy the timestamp string to the API call path to delete the API token.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the moment when you created the API key in the ISO 8601 compliant format.

parameters.name: notificationId
parameters.description: The ID of the notification type. Get the mapping of the `notificationId` and the team notification setting type with the API call `GET /enums/user-email-notifications`.

put.description:
Updates team notification settings for the user identified with the `userId`, `teamId` and `notificationId`.

Get the mapping of the `notificationId` and the team notification setting type with the API call `GET /enums/user-email-notifications`.

Note that you can only update notification settings for the user associated with the API key used for authentication.

enabled.description: Enables or disables team notification type for the user.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The ID of the user.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The ID of the team.

parameters.name: notificationId
parameters.description: The ID of the notification type. Get the mapping of the `notificationId` and the team notification setting type with the API call `GET /enums/user-email-notifications`.

get.description:
Returns users who have a specific permission within an organization or team.

Use `type: organization` with `organizationId` to find users that hold a given organization-level permission.
Use `type: company` with `teamId` to find users that hold a given team-level permission.

Requires the `organization users view` permission on the resolved organization.
When `type` is `company`, additionally requires the `team view` permission on the specified team.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Whether to filter by an organization-level or team-level (company) permission.
When `organization`, supply `organizationId`.
When `company`, supply `teamId`.

parameters.name: permission
parameters.description:
The permission string to filter by. Must be a valid permission for the given `type`.
For example `organization users view` (organization type) or `team view` (company type).

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: Required when `type` is `organization`. The organization to scope the query to.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: Required when `type` is `company`. The team to scope the query to.

parameters.name: cols[]
parameters.description:
Specifies columns that are returned in the response. Use the `cols[]` parameter for every column that you want to return in the response. For example `GET /endpoint?cols[]=key1&cols[]=key2` to get both `key1` and `key2` columns in the response.

[Check the "Filtering" section for a full example.](/api-documentation/pagination-sorting-filtering/filtering)

id.description: Unique identifier of the user.

name.description: Full name of the user.

privateSpaceId.description: ID of the user's private space (team) within the organization, if any.

language.description: Preferred language code of the user.

avatar.description: URL of the user's avatar image.

lastLogin.description: Timestamp of the user's last login.

invitationStatus.description:
Status of the user's organization invitation.
`pending` — the user has been invited but has not yet accepted; the invitation is still valid.
`expired` — the invitation has not been accepted in time.
`null` — the user is a fully accepted member (no outstanding invitation).

400.description: Bad request — invalid or missing parameters.

403.description: Forbidden — the authenticated user does not have the required permissions.

post.description: Creates a new custom organization or team role. Requires the `organization custom roles edit` permission on the organization.

name.description: The name of the custom role.

permissionType.description: Whether the role applies to the organization or a team.

organizationId.description: The ID of the organization to create the custom role in.

description.description: An optional description of the custom role.

permissions.description: List of permission IDs to assign to the custom role.

id.description: The unique ID of the custom role.

name.description: The name of the custom role.

category.description: Whether the role applies to the organization or a team.

managementType.description: The management type of the role. Always `custom_managed` for custom roles.

description.description: The description of the custom role.

permissions.description: List of permissions assigned to the custom role.

patch.description: Updates the name, description, or permissions of an existing custom role. Only custom-managed roles can be updated.

id.description: The ID of the custom role to update.

organizationId.description: The ID of the organization the custom role belongs to.

name.description: New name for the custom role.

description.description: New description for the custom role. Pass `null` to clear it.

permissions.description: Full list of permission IDs to assign to the role. Replaces existing permissions.

id.description: The unique ID of the custom role.

name.description: The name of the custom role.

category.description: Whether the role applies to the organization or a team.

managementType.description: The management type of the role. Always `custom_managed` for custom roles.

description.description: The description of the custom role.

permissions.description: List of permissions assigned to the custom role.

delete.description: Deletes an existing custom role. Only custom-managed roles can be deleted. The role must not be currently assigned to any users.

id.description: The ID of the custom role to delete.

organizationId.description: The ID of the organization the custom role belongs to.

roleId.description: The ID of the deleted custom role.

get.description: Gets list of all existing user role names and IDs. Optionally filter by `category`, a specific `roleId`, or pass `organizationId`/`teamId` to include custom roles for that organization. Set the user roles in an organization with the `POST /users/{userId}/user-organization-roles/{organizationId}` API call. Use the `POST /users/{userId}/user-team-roles/{teamId}` API call to set user roles in a team.

parameters.name: cols[]
parameters.description:
Specifies columns that are returned in the response. Use the `cols[]` parameter for every column that you want to return in the response. For example `GET /endpoint?cols[]=key1&cols[]=key2` to get both `key1` and `key2` columns in the response.

[Check the "Filtering" section for a full example.](/api-documentation/pagination-sorting-filtering/filtering)

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Set this parameter to `organization` or `team` to get user roles in an organization or in a team.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: Include custom roles belonging to this organization. Can be combined with `teamId`; if both are provided and the team does not belong to the organization, the request returns `400`. If the organization does not have the `customRoles` license, or the caller lacks permission to view that organization's users, custom roles are silently excluded from the response while the system roles are still returned.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: Include custom roles belonging to the organization that owns this team. Can be combined with `organizationId`; if both are provided and the team does not belong to the organization, the request returns `400`. If the organization does not have the `customRoles` license, or the caller lacks permission to view that organization's users, custom roles are silently excluded from the response while the system roles are still returned.

parameters.name: roleId
parameters.description: Filter the response to a single role by its ID.

parameters.name: excludeRole[]
parameters.description: Exclude roles with these IDs from the response.

managementType.description: Indicates how the role is managed.

list.description: Get all existing user role IDs and the corresponding role names.

all-cols.description: Get all existing user role IDs and the corresponding role names. The response contains all columns available in the endpoint.

usersRoles.name: Team Admin
usersRoles.description: Team Admin has full access to all data within the team. Only team admins can create custom functions.

usersRoles.name: Team Monitoring
usersRoles.description: Team Monitoring has read-only access to the team, scenarios, and templates.

400.description: Bad request. Returned when `organizationId` and `teamId` are both provided and the team does not belong to the specified organization.

get.description: Returns the detail of a single role by its ID. For custom-managed roles, requires the `customRoles` license and `ORGANIZATION_USERS_VIEW` permission on the role's organization.

parameters.name: roleId
parameters.description: The ID of the role.

400.description: Not available on self-hosted (private) deployments.

403.description: Insufficient rights or the organization does not have the `customRoles` license.

get.description: Gets a list of all available permissions that can be assigned to user roles. Use the `roleCategory` parameter to filter permissions by team or organization roles.

parameters.name: roleCategory
parameters.description: Filter permissions by role category. Use `team` for team role permissions or `organization` for organization role permissions.

id.description: Unique identifier of the permission.

name.description: Name of the permission.

category.description: Functional category the permission belongs to.

roleCategory.description: Whether this permission applies to team roles or organization roles.

label.description: A key to the dictionary on imt-web-zone for the permission label.

customRolesHidden.description: If true, the permission is hidden from custom role configuration and cannot be assigned to a custom role.

post.description:
Updates the specified user role in the organization. Only organization owners and admins can change the user organization roles. Refer to the Make Help center for a breakdown of the user [role permissions](https://www.make.com/en/help/access-management/organizations#organization-roles).

Specify the ID of the new role for the user in the request body. Get all available user role IDs and the corresponding user role names with the API call `GET /users/roles`.

You cannot change the organization "Owner" with this endpoint. Use the API call to [transfer organization ownership](./post--users--userid--user-organization-roles--organizationid--transfer.md) instead.

If you send an empty request body, the user with the specified `userId` will be removed from the organization.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The ID of the user.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.

parameters.name: cols[]
parameters.description:
Specifies columns that are returned in the response. Use the `cols[]` parameter for every column that you want to return in the response. For example `GET /endpoint?cols[]=key1&cols[]=key2` to get both `key1` and `key2` columns in the response.

[Check the "Filtering" section for a full example.](/api-documentation/pagination-sorting-filtering/filtering)

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Use this parameter when you are removing a user from an organization. Set this parameter to `true` is you want to delete the user's connections from the organization with the parameter `deleteConnections`.

parameters.name: deleteConnections
parameters.description: Set this parameter to `true` if you are removing a user from an organization to delete also the user's connections. If you set this parameter to `false`, the API call won't delete the user's connections.

usersRoleId.description: The ID of the user role. Check the `GET /users/roles` API call for the available `usersRoleId` values.

set-user-role.description: Set the user role in the organization. Get all user role IDs with the API call `GET /users/roles`.

all-cols.description: Set user role in the organization. The response returns all available columns for the user role in the organization.

remove-user.description: To remove a user from the organization send empty request body.

post.description: Transfer organization ownership to the user with the specified `userId`. Only organization owner can transfer their ownership to another user.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The ID of the user.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The ID of the organization.
